entities.name: Aberrant Form

prerequisite.other: Stage 1 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}

entries: Your creature type becomes Aberration in addition to any other creature types you have.

Also, the first time you become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} after a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your current Transformation Stage.

entities.name: Aberrant Mutation

prerequisite.other: Stage 1 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}

entries: Your body can twist and reshape itself as you will it, changing body parts into dangerous or useful tools and regenerating after taking damage. These abilities are represented by Aberrant Mutations. You can manifest an Aberrant Mutation listed below a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your current Transformation Stage.

Each of these Aberrant Mutations last for 1 minute, and you can end them early by spending a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to remove them or take on a different mutation. If you choose another Aberrant Mutation, you must spend another use of this feature.
You regain all uses of this ability when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Chitinous Shell
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you grow a hard, crustacean-like shell. While this mutation is active and are not wearing heavy armor, your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} increases by 2. While you maintain this shell, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet.

entries.name: Eldritch Limbs
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you transform one or both of your arms into thick muscle, scything claws, or sharpened bone. When you use the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can replace or or more attacks with melee attacks made with your eldritch limb. You are considered proficient with this attack, and it uses either Strength or Dexterity (your choice). On a hit, the attack deals {@damage 1d8} damage (either Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing, chosen each time you manifest the mutation).
As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can make a melee attack with your eldritch limb.
Your eldritch limbs cannot hold weapons, shields, or other items. They are not considered weapons or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}.

entries.name: Slimy Form
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you cover yourself in a slippery slime. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks to escape a grapple, and you can use the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You also gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Acid, Fire, and Cold damage while in this form.

entities.name: Aberration
entries: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the Aberration creature type. If you are already proficient in a skill when asked to make an Intelligence or Wisdom ability check relating to Aberrations, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} instead.
When you gain this feature, you also learn {@language Deep Speech} or {@language Undercommon}.

entities.name: Abyssal Resistance
entries: You gain {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the damage type chosen for {@optfeature Fiendish Soul|GrimHollowPG24}, and you gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the other two damage types. Additionally, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to damage from nonmagical weapon attacks or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}.

entities.name: Acid Blood (Aberration, Ooze, Plant)
entries: For 1 minute, your blood becomes acidic. Each time a creature hits you with an attack while within 5 feet of you, it takes {@damage 2d6} Acid damage.

entities.name: Acolyte of Undeath
entries: When you reduce a Humanoid to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and it dies, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to immediately transform it into a {@creature Zombie|XMM} under your control. You can have three such creatures under your control at once. These Zombies act on their own {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, following your verbal commands to the best of their ability.

entities.name: Acumen (Celestial, Fiend, Fey)
entries: For 1 hour, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) checks. Additionally, choose either {@skill Intimidation|XPHB} or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks using the chosen skill.

entities.name: Adept of the Green Sisterhood
entries: As your connection to your Green Sisterhood grows, your body and your magical skills strengthen.

items: You gain a Claw attack that deals {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing Damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

You can hide yourself from others. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can cast {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Adept of the Red Sisterhood
entries: As your connection to your Red Sisterhood grows, your body and your magical skills strengthen.

items: You gain a Claw attack that deals {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing Damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

You can sway others to your will. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can cast {@spell Charm Person|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. The spell save DC when cast in this way is 12 plus your Transformation Stage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Adept of the Sea Sisterhood
entries: As your connection to your Sea Sisterhood grows, your body and your magical skills strengthen.

items: You gain a Claw attack that deals {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing Damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

You can appear as someone other than yourself. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can cast {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. The spell save DC when cast in this way is 12 plus your Transformation Stage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Adrenal Injection
entries: For 1 minute, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet. In addition, once on each of your turns, you can jump up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.

entities.name: Allsense Injection
entries: For 1 minute, you have {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet.

entities.name: Amphibious
entries: Surviving underwater is second nature to you. You can breathe air and water.

entries.name: Water Born
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks or saving throws made while submerged in water. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Angelic Wings
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can manifest feathered wings for 1 hour. While they are manifested, you have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} when you are not wearing heavy armor.
You can manifest these wings a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage, and you regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Animal Friend
entries: Time spent among beasts has gifted you a way with those creatures. You have proficiency in the {@skill Animal Handling|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Animal Ally
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Animal Handling|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Arcane Eye Oil
entries: For 1 hour, you sense the presence of magical effects within 30 feet of yourself. If you sense such effects, you can take the {@action Magic|XPHB} action to see a faint aura around any visible creature or object in the area that bears the magic, and if an effect was created by a spell, you learn the spell's school of magic.
This sense is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

entities.name: Arch-Crone's Hunger
entries: You develop a taste for strange, cursed, or vile foods, and normal food no longer nourishes you. You must consume one of these unusual foods at least once every day or you gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level, which cannot be removed until you feed your vile hunger. If you are forced to eat or drink normal food, you must succeed on a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or vomit it up and gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.
Examples of vile foods include the following:

items: The flesh of sapient creatures (Humanoids, Fey, or Celestials)
Magical ingredients (rare herbs, dragon's blood, etc.)
The pain and suffering of others
Pages of a cursed tome

entities.name: Artifice Expertise
entries: Working with detritus and shattered objects has granted you an affinity for repairing and remaking things. You have proficiency with {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB}. (This is an Exploration trait.) Additionally, you can use your {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB} and 10 GP worth of appropriate materials to spend 10 minutes creating a small clockwork device. The device must fit in the palm of your hand, and can serve one of the following functions:

entries.name: {@style Smoker|small}
entries: The device exudes smoke in a 5-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} for 1 minute. Any objects or creatures within this {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} are considered {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB}.

entries.name: {@style Lighter|small}
entries: The device emits a small flame the size of a candle's that can light flammable objects.

entries.name: {@style Compass|small}
entries: The device always points north, or in a cardinal direction of the GM's determination on another plane.

entries.name: Expert Gadgeteer
entries: If you take this trait twice, you can make a device in 1 minute instead of 10 minutes. In addition, you can choose to imbue a device with the following extra function: (This is a Combat trait.)

entries.name: {@style Distractor|small}
entries: This device is set with blinking lights that can captivate other creatures. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you place or toss the device into a space within 30 feet of you. A creature sharing a space with the device must succeed on a DC 10 Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, attacks against that creature have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} until the start of your next turn. A creature can use an action to destroy the device. You can give up to three of your devices the Distractor feature. You regain the ability to do so when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Artificial Form
entries: You were made, not born, and your unnatural origin forever marks you as different. You are a Construct, but your enchanted form still benefits from healing spells. You can also heal yourself by spending {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice} during {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short Rests} and {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Long Rests}, as normal.
You don't need to eat, drink, sleep, or breathe. You must still be inactive for 8 hours during a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} to gain its benefits.

entries.name: Self-Repair
entries: If you take this trait twice, when the {@spell Mending|XPHB} cantrip is cast on you, you can spend a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Die} to regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll of the die plus your Constitution modifier (minimum 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Artisanal Focus
entries: You revere the crafting skill of ancestors long dead. Choose an {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB}. You have proficiency with that tool.

entries.name: Artisanal Expertise
entries: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain proficiency with a new tool each time.

Additionally, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks made using any tool you selected with Artisanal Focus. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Athlete's Spirit
entries: Your reserves of physical power have kept you alive on more than one occasion. You have proficiency in the {@skill Athletics|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Athlete's Resolve
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Athletics checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Aura of Awakening
entries: You emit an aura of power that awakens the elemental forces in your companions. When you first gain this feature, choose one of the options below. You may change the aura you emit upon finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Light as Air
entries: Whenever you or an ally you can see within 30 feet of you makes a Dexterity saving throw, the creature gains a bonus equal to your Constitution modifier.

entries.name: Forged in Fire
entries: When an ally within 10 feet of you deals damage with a melee attack, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make the attack deal Fire damage instead of the normal damage type, and add {@damage 2d6} Fire damage to the attack's damage.

entries.name: Heart of Stone
entries: You can choose to have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on any {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} check. If you do, as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you and each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@dice 1d10} plus your Constitution modifier. While a creature has {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} gained in this manner, they cannot have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition unless it is part of the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Fluid Movement
entries: When an ally within 30 feet moves or attempts to escape a Grapple, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to give them the following benefits:

items: Their {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet until the end of their turn.
Their movement doesn't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.
They have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on checks to escape the Grapple.

entities.name: Aura of Holy Purge
entries: You emit an aura of righteous fervor in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} centered on you, except when you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition. When an ally within your aura hits with a weapon attack, the ally can use their {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cause the hit to be a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}. If they do, you and the ally each gain a level of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}. After an ally uses this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, that ally can't use it again until after they finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Aura of Righteous Mercy
entries: You emit an aura of peaceful resolution in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} centered on you, except when you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition. When an ally within your aura would be reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, they can use their {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead. If they do, you and the ally each gain a level of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}. After an ally uses this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, that ally can't use it again until after they finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Awakened Mind
entries: The dangers of Etharis have given you a focus that allows you to shrug off debilitating magical effects. You automatically succeed on saving throws against magical effects that would give you the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, {@condition Stunned|XPHB}, or {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} conditions. This does not include effects that leave you {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} because you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

entries.name: Reawakened
entries: If you take this trait twice, you also have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.

entities.name: Awareness (All)
entries: For 10 minutes, you know the location of creatures that are the same type as the creature you consumed within 60 feet of you that are not behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}.

entities.name: Awesome Critical
entries: When fortune favors your blade, you know how to make it count. When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} with a melee attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can roll one of the weapon's damage dice one additional time and add it to the extra damage of the {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}.

entries.name: Maximum Critical
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you use Awesome Critical, you can add the maximum of the weapon's original damage dice and the extra Awesome Critical die to the extra damage of the {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}, rather than rolling them. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Barbarian
entries: You've learned to fight with primal ferocity.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while you aren't wearing Heavy armor, you can enter a rampage for 1 minute. During this time, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage, and you can't maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}. Your rampage ends early if you use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} or have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.
Once you use this talent, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Battlefield Control
entries: When foes attempt to press you in melee, they do so at their peril. Other creatures provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} from you whenever they move into your reach, in addition to when they move out of your reach.

entries.name: Battlefield Dominance
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Beacon to Darkness
entries: You are now a Seraph of noted virtue and divinity, becoming a target for the world's evil. Whenever you or an ally within 30 feet commits an evil act, such as murdering innocents, you acquire a trace of darkness and you are Corrupted. GMs determine what constitutes an evil act.

While you are Corrupted, whenever you make an attack roll against an evil creature or a saving throw against a spell or effect from an evil creature, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the check. Also, if an ally is the one who caused your Corruption, they cannot be considered your ally or you theirs until the Corruption is removed.

You can remove the Corrupted condition by finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, during which you must pray for at least 1 hour and burn holy incense or donate wealth to a local good cause worth at least 100 GP times your Transformation Stage.

entities.name: Beast
entries: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the Beast creature type. If you are already proficient in a skill when asked to make an Intelligence or Wisdom ability check relating to Beasts, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} instead.
When you gain this feature, you also learn {@language Sylvan}.

entities.name: Beguiler's Charm
entries: You gain the ability to manipulate the mind of a creature with your unearthly charm. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can choose a Humanoid or Beast within 30 feet of you that can see you. The creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw with a DC of 8 plus your Charisma modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for 24 hours. At the end of the 24 hours, you can choose to automatically renew the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition on that creature with no saving throw. You may only have one creature {@condition Charmed|XPHB} in this manner at a time. If you use this feature on another creature, or you or an ally damages the creature, the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition ends on the previous creature immediately.

entities.name: Bestial Vigor
entries: Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} Maximum increases by an amount equal to your character level, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a character level.
Additionally, when in your hybrid form, you gain 5 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of each of your turns.

entities.name: Binding Curse
entries: You can compel a creature with a binding curse. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose a creature within 30 feet who can see you. That creature makes a Charisma saving throw against a DC of 12 plus your Transformation Stage. On a failed save it cannot move more than 30 feet away from you for 1 minute. Also, during that time, your weapon attacks and {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} against the creature deal an additional {@dice 2d6} Necrotic damage.
You can use this feature three times, regaining all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Blinding Radiance
entries: Your Transformation into a Seraph brings with it changes to your physical features. Your body radiates a divine glow, your eyes may become hollow braziers of celestial light, or your many sets of wings may appear unsettling to mortal creatures.

You can suspend this form and manifest the appearance of the humanoid you once were, but this is taxing and requires effort. This form is not permanent, and moments of stress or wrath are likely to reveal your true nature. This might occur under the following circumstances:

items: Becoming {@condition Bloodied|XPHB}
Having the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition
Witnessing an act of evil
Encountering Fiendish creatures or powers
Using your Seraph Transformation powers for the first time after a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}

entries: In these events, or times of other extreme emotional or physical stress, a GM can call  or a Constitution saving throw with a DC based on your current Transformation Stage.  If you fail this save, your Blinding Radiance is revealed.

When your true nature is revealed this way, all non-enemy creatures within 30 feet of you who can see you must succeed on a DC 15 Charisma saving throw or have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. Additionally, for the next minute, attacks against you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

entities.name: Born Healer
entries: When others suffer, you are there to help. You have proficiency in the {@skill Medicine|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Combat Doctor
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Medicine|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Bow of Celestial Domination
entries: While your Bow of Celestial Judgement is manifested, you gain these additional benefits:

items: When you use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to target a creature you can see within 120 feet, you can also affect one other creature with 10 feet of the original target.
You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Necrotic damage instead of {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}.
You gain 15 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of each of your turns instead of 5.

entities.name: Bow of Celestial Judgement
entries: You can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to manifest a powerful bow made of divine light. The Bow of Celestial Judgement lasts for 1 minute and grants you the following abilities while manifested:

items: You can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to target a creature you can see within 120 feet with your bow. The creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw against a DC equal to 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 6d6} Radiant damage, or {@dice 10d6} if it is a Fey, Fiend, or Undead. On a success, the creature takes half damage.
You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage.
You gain 5 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of each of your turns.

entries: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage, and you regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Brave
entries: The horrors you've lived through have hardened you. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid being {@condition Frightened|XPHB}.

entries.name: Infectious Bravery
entries: If you take this trait twice, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to bolster the spirits of your allies, granting one ally who can see or hear you {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on a saving throw against being {@condition Frightened|XPHB}. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Breath Weapon
entries: A connection to draconic or elemental fury lets you unleash a blast of destructive energy. When you select this trait, choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder. Then choose an area of effect: a {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} that is 5 feet wide and 30 feet long, or a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}.

When you use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to expel your Breath Weapon, each creature in the area of effect must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your Constitution modifier + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). A target creature takes {@damage 1d8} damage of the chosen type on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. This damage increases by {@dice 1d8} when you reach character levels 5 ({@dice 2d8}), 11 ({@dice 3d8}), and 17 ({@dice 4d8}).
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Potent Breath
entries: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain an additional breath weapon each time, with its own number of uses, damage type, and area of effect. Additionally, when you use any of your Breath Weapons, one target of your choice has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Burst of Speed
entries: The many things that want to kill you must catch you first. On your turn, you can increase your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} by 30 feet until the end of your turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Furious Speed
entries: If you take this trait twice, on a turn when you use Burst of Speed, you don't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

entities.name: Calculating Listener
entries: The weak-willed around you are easy targets for your manipulation. By conversing with a nonhostile creature for at least 1 minute, you can attempt to charm them. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your Charisma modifier + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for 1 hour. At the GM's discretion, you also learn one piece of information that the target knows that relates to the topic of conversation while you speak to them. Regardless of whether or not the target succeeds on the saving throw, they remain unaware of your attempt. You regain use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Master Manipulator
entries: If you take this trait twice, a creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw, and it has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for 8 hours on a failed save.

entities.name: Call of Unmaking
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can give a mournful wail or otherwise set up a wracking vibration. This sound has no effect on Constructs and Undead.

All other creatures of your choice within 30 feet of you must make a Constitution saving throw. The DC of the Constitution saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage. Creatures that can't hear you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the saving throw.
On a failed save, the creature bears the Mark of Unmaking for 1 minute. While bearing this mark, a creature takes an additional {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage each time it takes damage.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Celestial
entries: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the Celestial creature type. If you are already proficient in a skill when asked to make an Intelligence or Wisdom ability check relating to Celestials, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} instead.
When you gain this feature, you also learn {@language Celestial}.

entities.name: Celestial Form
entries: You become a Celestial in addition to any other creature type(s) you are. Additionally, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Radiant Damage.

entities.name: Centered
entries: By focusing your inner strength, you gain a needed edge. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you grant yourself {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on an attack roll or ability check you make before the start of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Centered Edge
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you succeed on the attack roll or ability check made while using Centered, you can choose one creature within 30 feet of you. That creature has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next attack roll or ability check they make before the start of your next turn.

entities.name: Charging Attack
entries: The fury with which you throw yourself into battle forces your foes to feel your wrath. If you move at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hit it with a melee attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} on the same turn, you can make another attack against the same target as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} with the same weapon.

entries.name: Furious Charge
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you use Charging Attack, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all attacks after the triggering movement until the end of your turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Cleanse Affliction
entries: When you use Divine Clemency on a creature, they gain the following additional benefits:

items: They gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the amount of damage healed by {@variantrule Healing|XPHB} Word.
They have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on their next {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} made within the next minute.
You end one of the following conditions on it: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, or {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}.

entities.name: Cleric
entries: You've gained the ability to channel divine energy directly from the gods, emboldening allies and sanctifying their attacks. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose a creature within 30 feet of you. For 10 minutes, the target gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half your Bard level plus your Charisma modifier. While a creature has {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} granted by this talent, it can choose to lose all remaining {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} when it hits a creature with an attack made with a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, dealing extra Radiant damage equal to the number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} lost.
Once you use this talent, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Climber
entries: Sometimes staying away from what threatens you means getting clear of those threats. You have a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

entries.name: Wall Walker
entries: If you take this trait twice, you can use your {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} to move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and upside down along ceilings, while leaving your hands free.

Additionally, while using climbing movement, you can use the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Commanding Insight
entries: Those who attempt to deceive you do so in vain. You have proficiency in the {@skill Insight|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Exceptional Insight
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Insight checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Connection to Nature
entries: You've learned that paying attention to the environment around you is the best way to predict its threats. You have proficiency in the {@skill Nature|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Bond with Nature
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Nature checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Constricting Tendrils

prerequisite.other: Stage 3 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}

entries: You gain an additional option for your Writing Tendrils mutation.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while Writhing Tentacles is active, you can attempt to restrain a creature within 5 feet of you with your tendrils. The creature must succeed on a Strength or Dexterity saving throw or gain the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition. The DC is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage. A {@condition Restrained|XPHB} creature can take an action to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against this DC, ending the condition on itself on a success. The {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition ends if you are {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, you move from your current position, if you retract your tendrils, or if you use your tendrils to perform another ability.

entities.name: Construct
entries: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the Construct creature type. If you are already proficient in a skill when asked to make an Intelligence or Wisdom ability check relating to Constructs, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} instead.
When you gain this feature, you also learn {@language Draconic}.

entities.name: Corrosive Membrane
entries: When you manifest your Ooze Form, it lasts for 10 minutes.

While manifesting your Ooze Form, you are covered with a sheen of acidic slime. When a creature hits you with a melee attack, it takes Acid damage equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your Transformation Stage. Nonmagical ammunition is destroyed immediately after hitting you and dealing damage. Any nonmagical weapon takes a cumulative -1 penalty to attack rolls immediately after dealing damage or coming into contact with you. The weapon is destroyed if the penalty reaches -5. The penalty can be removed by casting the {@spell Mending|XPHB} spell on the weapon.
Additionally, you can eat through 2 feet of nonmagical wood or metal in 1 minute simply by touching it.

entities.name: Corrupting Magic
entries: Whenever you kill any creature with a spell, you may apply an additional effect depending on the damage dealt. This ability cannot be used when you kill a Construct, Undead, or any creature without a soul.
Choose one of the following effects when you use this ability:

entries.name: Life Force
entries: As the soul of your enemy leaves their body, you siphon off part of their life force. You regain {@dice 1d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} per Transformation Stage.

entries.name: Poison
entries: Choose a creature within 10 feet of the killed creature. That creature takes {@damage 1d6} Poison damage per Transformation Stage.

entries.name: Exhaustion
entries: Choose a creature within 10 feet of the killed creature. That creature gains one {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level. A creature may only have one {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level from this feature at a time.

entities.name: Crafter's Eye
entries: The history of Etharis is written in relics, and you read that history better than most. When you make a {@skill History|XPHB} check related to any object (an item, device, building, or material) and you have proficiency in an {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} associated with creating that object, you are considered proficient in {@skill History|XPHB} and you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the check instead of your normal bonus.

entries.name: Crafter's Cunning
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the {@skill History|XPHB} checks you make with Crafter's Eye. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Creature Cover
entries: By slipping behind enemies or allies alike, you are able to fade from view with ease. You can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action even when you have {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} from a creature, as long as that creature is of a size larger than you.

entries.name: Subtle Cover
entries: If you take this trait twice, you can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action when you have {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} from a creature the same size as you.

entities.name: Creature of Earth
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 3 Mutation Points}
You have {@sense Tremorsense|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet. You can spend 2 additional Mutation Points to gain a {@variantrule Burrow Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

entities.name: Creature of the Sea
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 1 Mutation Point}

You gain a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and you can breathe underwater. For an additional 1 Mutation Point, you gain a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to twice your speed.

entities.name: Creature of the Sky
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 3 Mutation Points}
You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet. For an additional 2 Mutation Points, your {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} increases to 60 feet.

entities.name: Curse of the Befuddled
entries: {@b Cost:} 2 Jinx Points
As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose a creature that you can see within 60 feet of yourself that is cursed by your Evil Eye and spend 2 Jinx Points. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw.

On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for 10 minutes or until you or your allies damage it. When the effect ends, the target knows it was {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by you.
After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.

entities.name: Curse of the Clumsy
entries: {@b Cost:} 3 Jinx Points

When a creature cursed by your Evil Eye moves at least 5 feet on its turn, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and spend 3 Jinx Points to cause the creature to have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and have its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} reduced to 0 until the end of its turn.
After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.

entities.name: Curse of the Debilitated
entries: {@b Cost:} 1 Jinx Point
When a creature cursed by your Evil Eye takes damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and spend 1 Jinx Point to roll {@dice 1d12}. The creature takes Necrotic damage equal to the roll.
After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.

entities.name: Curse of the Doomed
entries: {@b Cost:} 1 Jinx Point
When you miss with an attack roll against a creature cursed by your Evil Eye, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and spend 1 Jinx Point to reroll the attack. You must use the new roll.
After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.

entities.name: Curse of the Fearful
entries: {@b Cost:} 2 Jinx Points
As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose a creature that you can see within 60 feet of yourself that is cursed by your Evil Eye and spend 2 Jinx Points. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw.

On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} target repeats the save, ending the Misfortune on itself on a success.
After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.

entities.name: Curse of the Inept
entries: {@b Cost:} 1 Jinx Point

After a creature you can see that is cursed by your Evil Eye makes a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and spend 1 Jinx Point to make the target reroll, and it must use the new roll.
After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.

entities.name: Curse of the Insensate
entries: {@b Cost:} 3 Jinx Points
As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose a creature that you can see that is cursed by your Evil Eye and spend 3 Jinx Points. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw.

On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Deafened|XPHB} conditions for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the Misfortune on itself on a success.
After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.

entities.name: Curse of the Maimed
entries: {@b Cost:} 2 Jinx Points

When you hit a creature cursed by your Evil Eye with an attack roll using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} and roll a 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and spend 2 Jinx Points to score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}.
After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.

entities.name: Curse of the Marked
entries: {@b Cost:} 2 Jinx Points

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can spend 2 Jinx Points to increase the duration of your Evil Eye to 1 hour or until you mark a different creature with your Evil Eye, whichever comes first.
Additionally, while a creature is affected by this curse, you always know the direction and distance to the creature while the two of you are on the same plane of existence.

entities.name: Curse of the Plagued
entries: {@b Cost:} 1 Jinx Point

When a creature you can see that is cursed by your Evil Eye would regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and spend 1 Jinx Point to halve the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} regained. Afterward, the creature can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} until the start of your next turn.
After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.

entities.name: Curse of the Somnolent
entries: {@b Cost:} 3 Jinx Points
As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose a creature you can see that is cursed by your Evil Eye and spend 3 Jinx Points. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw.

On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The condition ends on a target if it takes damage or someone within 5 feet of it takes an action to shake it out of the Misfortune's effect.
Creatures that don't sleep, such as elves, or that have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} condition automatically succeed on saves against this Misfortune.
After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.

entities.name: Curse of the Unlucky
entries: {@b Cost:} 3 Jinx Points

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can spend 3 Jinx Points to put a pox on a creature cursed by your Evil Eye. Whenever the creature is about to make an attack roll or saving throw, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and subtract the number rolled from the total.
This Misfortune ends when the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.

entities.name: Cursed Claw

prerequisite.other: Stage 3 of the {@charoption Shadowsteel Ghoul|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation}

entries: You gain a Claw attack. This attack uses either your Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls.
You can use this attack twice as part of an {@action Attack|XPHB} action, or once as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. The Claw attack deals {@dice 2d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier.

Additionally, when the Claw attack hits, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be afflicted with a random Shadowsteel curse at Stage 1. The saving throw DC is 8 plus your Constitution modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. A creature already afflicted with a Shadowsteel curse cannot gain another Shadowsteel curse with this attack.

entities.name: Daemonic Brand
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn, you can brand a creature within 60 feet of you that you can see with a fiery mark that remains on them for 1 minute. If the target succeeds on a Wisdom saving throw, they are not branded. The DC of the saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.
If the creature fails the saving throw, you choose one of the following effects that lasts for the duration:

items: The creature takes a -2 penalty on all saving throws.
The first attack against the creature on each turn is made with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
The creature cannot regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

entries: You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Damage Resistance
entries: Exposure to the worst effects of a specific energy has given you a tolerance to its effects. You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to one of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder.

entries.name: Damage Immunity
entries: If you take this trait twice, as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to taking damage of the type you chose for {@variantrule Damage|XPHB} {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to that damage type until the end of your next turn. You regain the use this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Damage Resistance (Armor Modification)
entries: The monster hide used to craft your armor grants some resistances. You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to two of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder.

entities.name: Darkvision
entries: A life spent in shadow has made you grow accustomed to the gloom. You can see in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} within 60 feet of you as if it were {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}, and in {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} within 60 feet of you as if it were {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB}. You can't discern color in {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Improved Darkvision
entries: If you take this trait twice, the range of your {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} increases to 120 feet.

entities.name: Darkvision (CM)
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 1 Mutation Point}
You gain {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 60 feet.

entities.name: Debilitating Magic

prerequisite.other: Stage 1 of the {@charoption Shadowsteel Ghoul|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation}

entries: Choose one ability score. You lose 2 points in that ability as the power of the Shadowsteel slowly eats away at your body, soul, or both.

entities.name: Devilish Contractor
entries: You have acquired the ability to bind mortal creatures to your will. You can create a contract to bind a mortal Humanoid's soul to you, feeding on its power. The mortal gains a gift asked for in the contract, and you gain a Gift of Damnation of your choice (see Gifts of Damnation).
To bind a mortal's soul to you, you must first create a contract for their desired gift. A contract requires magical ink and paper worth 10 GP for each Transformation Stage you have acquired.

You and the mortal must both sign the contract willingly, fully aware of the costs involved. Once signed, daemonic entities give you a Gift of Damnation, and the mortal receives their desired wishes within 7 days. You do not have to provide this boon yourself.

For example, upon signing a contract for a {@optfeature Gift of Unfettered Glory|GrimHollowPG24}, you receive the benefits listed for that gift. The mortal who signs receives their stated desire, provided by the dubious powers of the Netherworld. You cannot benefit directly from the mortal's desired gift, nor can an ally. No ally of yours can ever sign a contract with you.
You may take a Gift of Damnation of the Stage that you have attained or lower. For example, if you are at Stage 2 of the Transformation, you may only take a Stage 1 or Stage 2 Gift of Damnation.

There is no limit to the number of contracts you can execute, but you can only have one active Gift of Damnation at a time. When you would gain a second Gift of Damnation, you may replace your active gift with the new one. Also, when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} you may swap your active Gift of Damnation for any other gift associated with a signed contract.

entities.name: Devilish Subcontractor
entries: You may gain the benefits of two Gifts of Damnation at the same time. You may still only switch one Gift of Damnation after a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Disquieting Voice
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 1 Mutation Point}
You can comprehend and verbally communicate with Beasts, and you can use any of the {@action Influence|XPHB} action's skill options with them.

entities.name: Divine Clemency
entries: When an ally within 30 feet of you that you can see takes damage, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cast {@spell Healing Word|XPHB} at first level on that ally without using a spell slot.
You can do this a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses of this feature after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Divine Sangromancy
entries: A connection to the life force of others lets you shape that force to their benefit. Whenever an allied creature within 30 feet of you regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can spend a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Die} and add the roll of the die to the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} gained by the ally.

entries.name: Sangromancy Savant
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you use Divine Sangromancy, you also regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Die} roll.

entities.name: Dragon
entries: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the Dragon creature type. If you are already proficient in a skill when asked to make an Intelligence or Wisdom ability check relating to Dragons, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} instead.
When you gain this feature, you also learn {@language Draconic}.

entities.name: Draining Attack
entries: As your enemy's life force ebbs, you grow ever stronger. If you have the {@optfeature Natural Attack|GrimHollowPG24} trait, each time you hit with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the damage dealt by the attack.

entries.name: To the Dregs
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you use Draining Attack, the target also takes a penalty to their {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum equal to the damage dealt by the attack. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Draining Flight

prerequisite.other: Stage 3 of the {@charoption Specter|GrimHollowPG24|Specter Transformation}, {@optfeature Incorporeal Movement|GrimHollowPG24} & {@optfeature Haunting Flight|GrimHollowPG24}

entries: You can now use your {@optfeature Incorporeal Movement|GrimHollowPG24} feature as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Additionally, your {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} while using {@optfeature Haunting Flight|GrimHollowPG24} becomes double your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

Additionally, while you are using {@optfeature Incorporeal Movement|GrimHollowPG24}, your movement does not provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} and you can damage creatures that you move through. Once per turn, when you enter a space occupied by a creature, you can choose to force that creature to succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 6d6} Psychic damage and have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. A creature takes half damage on a successful save and is not {@condition Frightened|XPHB}. The DC of the Constitution saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can use it again.

entities.name: Draught of Bear's Endurance
entries: For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution checks and Constitution saving throws.

entities.name: Draught of Bull's Strength
entries: For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.

entities.name: Draught of Cat's Grace
entries: For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity checks and Dexterity saving throws.

entities.name: Draught of Eagle's Splendor
entries: For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma checks and Charisma saving throws.

entities.name: Draught of Fox's Cunning
entries: For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence checks and Intelligence saving throws.

entities.name: Draught of Owl's Wisdom
entries: For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom checks and Wisdom saving throws.

entities.name: Drawn to Darkness
entries: Your body and soul are bound to the cold darkness of the realms of death. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} as your life force is drawn to oblivion.

entities.name: Dread Venom
entries: A foul-smelling blade oil that causes wounds to hemorrhage and refuse healing. Dread Venom can coat one Slashing or Piercing weapon or up to 10 pieces of Slashing or Piercing ammunition. Applying the oil takes 1 minute and the oil lasts 8 hours once applied. When a creature takes damage from the poisoned item, it can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} until it finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Dreams and Nightmares
entries: You gain the ability to manipulate the mind of mortal beings. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose a Humanoid you can see within 30 feet of you. That creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw. The DC is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.

On a failed saving throw, the creature has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition, as it is stuck in a blissful dream or a terrifying nightmare. The condition lasts for 1 minute. The target can attempt the Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns and each time it takes damage.

You can choose to concentrate on the effect as you would a spell. If you are {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on the effect when the target attempts its saving throw, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw.
You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses of this ability when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Dreamwalking
entries: Whenever you rest, you touch the dreams of those around you, seeding their thoughts and memories into your own mind. When you make an ability check to recall lore or knowledge, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Secret Dreams
entries: If you take this trait twice, you gain an instinctive knowledge of the secrets of other creatures while you touch their dreams. Using a {@action Search|XPHB} action, you focus on one creature you can see and make a DC 15 Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) check. With a successful check, you learn one secret of the GM's choice known to that creature. The secrets of creatures that don't have a language come to you as vague images and impressions. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Driver
entries: The roads and waterways of Etharis are often no less dangerous than the open wilderness, and you dedicate yourself to moving others safely on those routes. You have proficiency with {@item Navigator's Tools|XPHB}, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks made to drive a vehicle.

entries.name: Remarkable Driver
entries: If you take this trait twice, you can make checks involving driving a vehicle that require an action without having to use your action. You can only get this free use once per round.

entities.name: Druid
entries: You can cast the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell without expending a spell slot. When you cast the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell with this talent, you can target yourself only and the maximum {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} of the Beast you can transform into equals half your Druid level.
Once you cast the spell using this talent, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Dual Nature
entries: You add a second element to your {@optfeature Elemental Affinity|GrimHollowPG24|Elemental Affinity Boon}. When you do so, you gain the associated benefits of your new element.

entities.name: Eager Deceiver
entries: You long ago learned that being open with others only gives them power over you. You have proficiency in the {@skill Deception|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Expert Deceiver
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Deception checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Echolocation
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 3 Mutation Points}
You have {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet while you don't have the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition.

entities.name: Efficient Killer

prerequisite.other: Stage 2 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}

entries: When you make an attack with your eldritch limbs, you can gain an added ability based on the damage type you have chosen.

entries.name: Piercing
entries: You may replace any attack made with your eldritch limb with a razor-sharp barb hurled at the target. You are considered proficient with this barb, which has a Range of 20/60, has the {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse} and {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} properties, and deals {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage on a hit.

entries.name: Bludgeoning
entries: You manifest a long tentacle, tipped with a hardened bone club. Your eldritch limb attack deals an additional {@damage 1d8} Bludgeoning damage. Also, the attack uses the {@itemMastery Slow|XPHB} Weapon Property, which you have Mastery with.

entries.name: Slashing
entries: Your arm forms a row of viciously hooked claws and talons. Your eldritch limb attack deals an additional {@damage 1d8} Slashing damage. Also, the attack uses the {@itemMastery Graze|XPHB} Weapon Property, which you have Mastery with.

entities.name: Elastic Limbs
entries: Your limbs have become elastic and tacky, allowing you to reach distant enemies and climb walls.

Your reach increases by 5 feet and you gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. You can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and along ceilings, while leaving your hands free. Creatures you are grappling have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) and Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) checks to escape.

entities.name: Eldritch Aberration

prerequisite.other: Stage 4 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}, Has Spell Slots

entries: You gain the ability to deliver jolts of magical energy with your eldritch limbs. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack using your eldritch limbs, you can expend a spell slot to deal additional damage equal to {@dice 1d6} per spell level expended. You can choose for this additional damage to be of any of the following types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Force, Lightning, or Thunder. If any of these extra dice show a 6 when rolled, the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

entities.name: Eldritch Assault
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 2 Mutation Points}
You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls you make with your Wild Shape form's attacks.

entries.name: Repeatable
entries: You can gain this Mutation more than once, but no more than three times.

entities.name: Eldritch Concentration
entries: Your supernatural ability with magic manifests in new ways. When you cast a spell that requires {@status Concentration|XPHB}, if you are already {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on one such spell, you can spend the charge of your soul vessel. If you do this, you do not lose {@status Concentration|XPHB} on the original spell. Instead, you gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.

If you cast a third {@status Concentration|XPHB} spell during this time, or lose {@status Concentration|XPHB} for any other reason, you lose {@status Concentration|XPHB} on both current spells you are {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on.
Once you use this feature you cannot use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Eldritch Omniscience
entries: You have better mastered the magical arts. The number of spells you can learn and can have prepared increases by 4. Additionally, whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} you gain 1 additional spell slot of whichever level you choose.
If a creature dies as a result of one of your spells, you can instantly draw that soul into your soul vessel without using a {@action Magic|XPHB} action or {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

entities.name: Elemental
entries: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the Elemental creature type. If you are already proficient in a skill when asked to make an Intelligence or Wisdom ability check relating to Elementals, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} instead.
When you gain this feature, you also learn {@language Primordial}.

entities.name: Elemental Affinity
entries: Though you have sway over all the elements, your Primordial change was sparked by one particular element: your Elemental Affinity. This element infuses your form and dictates your powers. You must choose one element below and gain all the benefits of that element:

items.name: Air
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage. In addition, you can channel the air currents around you to guide a ranged attack. Once on each of your turns, when you make an attack with a Ranged weapon or ranged spell, you can make that attack with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. If you are in a location without air—such as underwater or in a vacuum—this feature has no effect.

items.name: Earth
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning damage. In addition, whenever you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, you gain additional {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

items.name: Fire
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage. In addition, whenever you deal Fire damage, you can add your Constitution modifier to the damage dealt.

items.name: Water
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage. In addition, whenever a creature within 30 feet regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to imbue them with healing elemental energy. The creature regains an additional {@dice 1d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

entities.name: Elemental Ammunition
entries: The head of an arrow or bolt is tipped with poison, loaded with an acid vial, dipped in flammable oil, or coated with another substance. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you imbue with elemental power a single piece of ammunition that can be fired from a {@item Longbow|XPHB}, {@item Shortbow|XPHB}, or Crossbow as part of an {@action Attack|XPHB} action. When an Elemental Ammunition is imbued, choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder. When a creature takes damage from the Elemental Ammunition, it takes an additional {@damage 2d6} damage of the chosen type.

Elemental Ammunition discharges its energy if it hits, and it can't be recovered. If the attack misses, the Elemental Ammunition can be recovered and used again. Elemental Ammunition retains its power for 48 hours.

entities.name: Elemental Charge (Armor Modification)
entries: You embed your armor with a Construct's gemstone or infuse it with the power of an Elemental. Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder. When you hit a creature with an attack, you can cause it to deal the chosen damage type rather than its normal damage type, and the attack deals an extra {@dice 1d6} of that type.

entities.name: Elemental Imbalance
entries: Your body reacts in strange ways to the application of severe elemental damage. When you take Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage, roll {@dice 1d6}. On a 1, your Primordial form reacts in a volatile manner, and the following effects occur:

items: You gain {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to the instance of damage you just took, even if you had {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} or {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to that damage.
Creatures within 5 feet of you take the original amount of damage that you took. A DC 15 Constitution saving throw halves this damage.

entities.name: Elemental Inurement
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 2 Mutation Points}
You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to one of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder.

entries.name: Repeatable
entries: You can gain this Mutation more than once, but you must choose a different {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} each time.

entities.name: Elemental Mastery
entries: You add the final element to you Elemental Affinity Boon. When you do so, you gain the associated benefits of your new element.

Additionally, you can summon the four elements to wreathe you in power. Whenever a creature hits you with a melee attack, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to force it to make a Dexterity saving throw, dealing {@damage 6d6} damage of a type of your choice from Bludgeoning, Cold, Fire, or Lightning, or half as much on a successful save.
After you use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, your Elemental Affinity features gain the following benefits until the end of your next turn

entries.name: Air
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all Ranged weapon and ranged spell attacks.

entries.name: Earth
entries: At the beginning of your turn, you gain 20 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

entries.name: Fire
entries: At the beginning of your turn, each creature of your choice within 5 feet of you takes {@damage 2d6} Fire damage.

entries.name: Water
entries: Whenever a creature withing 60 feet that you can see regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} by spending {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} or through a spell or magical ability, that creature regains an additional 20 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier, regaining all uses upon completing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Elemental Surge
entries: You can channel pure elemental energy into a concentrated bolt of an element of your choice. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can use any of the following options:

items.name: Lightning Strike
entries: You can make a ranged attack targeting a creature within 60 feet. You are proficient with this attack, which uses your Constitution modifier. On a hit, this attack deals {@dice 3d8} plus your Constitution modifier Lightning damage. You can then use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to target another creature within 30 feet of the first target with the same attack.
Increase the damage of these attacks by {@dice 1d8} for each Transformation Stage above 2.

items.name: Earth Shard
entries: You can force a creature within 30 feet to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes Bludgeoning damage equal to {@dice 3d6} plus your Constitution modifier, or half as much on a successful save. Increase the damage by {@dice 1d6} for each Transformation Stage above 2.
You also gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the damage dealt.

items.name: Flame Wave
entries: Each creature in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} originating from you makes a Dexterity saving throw against a DC equal to 8 plus your Constitution modifier plus your Transformation Stage. On a failed save, creatures in the area take Fire damage equal to {@dice 2d8} plus your Constitution modifier. Increase the damage by {@dice 1d8} for each Transformation Stage above 2.

items.name: Aquatic Rejuvenation
entries: Choose a creature you can see within 60 feet of you. The creature regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d8} plus your Constitution modifier. Increase the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} regained by {@dice 1d8} for each Transformation Stage above 2.

items: You can use Elemental Surge a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier, regaining all expended uses upon finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Elfsight Oil
entries: For 8 hours, you have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 120 feet.

entities.name: Embrace the Past
entries: The lessons of the past are harsh, but learning those lessons might give you the best insight for navigating the future. You have proficiency in the {@skill History|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Deep Lore
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill History|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Enemy in Motion
entries: A lifetime spent wandering lets you judge when others' movement works to your benefit. When you make an attack roll against a creature or make a saving throw against a creature's attack, spell, or ability, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll or saving throw if that creature moved since the end of your last turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Moving Insight
entries: If you take this trait twice, Enemy in Motion also lets you use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to affect an ally's attack roll or saving throw if your ally is within 30 feet.

entities.name: Engulf
entries: When you manifest your Ooze Form, it lasts for 10 minutes.

While manifesting your Ooze Form, you can envelop your foes in your gooey body. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can try to engulf a creature of your size category or smaller that is within 5 feet of you. The target makes a Strength saving throw, with a DC equal to 10 plus your Strength modifier plus your Transformation Stage. On a failure, the creature is engulfed.

While engulfed, a target has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} against attacks and other effects outside of you, and when you move, the engulfed target moves with you. While engulfed, a creature takes {@damage 4d6} Acid damage at the start of each of its turns, is suffocating, has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition, and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. An engulfed creature that is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} is fully digested, leaving no body or nonmagical equipment behind.

entities.name: Enhanced Contract
entries: You may switch between your active Gift of Damnation at the end of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. In addition, when you switch Gifts of Damnation, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to 5 times your Transformation Stage.

entities.name: Enlarge
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 2 Mutation Points}

Your size increases by one category (from Medium to Large, for example). You can spend an additional 2 Mutation Points to increase your size by two categories instead (from Medium to Huge, for example), gaining the benefits of the spell again.
See the {@spell Enlarge/Reduce|XPHB} spell to determine the benefits gained from this ability.

entities.name: Entropic Abomination

prerequisite.other: Stage 4 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}

entries: This Flaw replacees the Stage 3 Flaw: Unstable Existence. The very essence of magic and the effect of stress aggravates the unstable nature of your aberrant body. Each time you fail a saving throw, or the first time you become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you must roll on the {@table Unstable Form|GrimHollowPG24|Unstable Form table}. If the result is less than your current Unstable Form effect, replace it with the new result.

entities.name: Environmental Awareness
entries: The natural world is a dangerous place, and your connection to specific parts of that world grants you an edge in survival. Choose an environment: arctic, coastal, desert, forest, grassland, hill and mountain, swamp, subterranean, or underwater. While in that environment, whenever you make an ability check to assess structures, monuments, or natural features; to find food or drinkable water; or to track creatures, you are considered to have proficiency in the appropriate skill for the check, and you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the check instead of your normal bonus.

entries.name: Adaptive Awareness
entries: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain its benefit for a new environment each time.

Additionally, when you make an ability check using Environmental Awareness, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Ethereal Fade
