entries: Shifting away from the mortal world lets you move through and observe that world unseen. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you fade from the Material Plane into the Ethereal Plane for 1 minute. While you remain in this state, you can't interact with the Material Plane, and effects on the Material Plane can't affect you, including spells and creatures. You can move and hear as normal, and you see everything in shades of gray. When the effect ends, you reappear in the Material Plane in the closest unoccupied space to where you faded from. You can end the effect early as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You regain the use of this feature again when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Ethereal Focus
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} when making Wisdom checks as part of a {@action Search|XPHB} action.

entities.name: Ethereal Phasing
entries: You can cast the {@spell Blink|XPHB} spell without expending a spell slot. You can cast the spell using this Boon a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage, and you regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

While you are under the effects of Blink when cast in this way, you also gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage at the start of each of your turns.

entities.name: Even in Sleep
entries: An instinctive sense for danger protects you at all times. While you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition while asleep, you are aware of your surroundings and can make {@skill Perception|XPHB} checks normally.

entries.name: Sleeping Ward
entries: If you take this trait twice, while you are asleep, you automatically detect the presence of any creature intending harm to you that moves within 30 feet of you. A creature that is simply capable of harming you does not trigger this trait until it has intent to do so. For example, a wild animal might approach you cautiously, then decide to attack only when it realizes you are sleeping.

entities.name: Evil Eye
entries: Your visage is so horrifying that you can cause creatures to drop dead when your gaze falls upon them. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you gaze upon a {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} Beast or Humanoid within 30 feet that can see your true form. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to 14 plus your Charisma modifier. If the target fails the save, it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. On a successful save, the creature takes {@damage 6d8} Psychic damage. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Eyes of the Night
entries: You gain {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} out to 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, the range increases by 60 feet.

entities.name: Fade Away
entries: You have learned to avoid notice at all costs, letting you momentarily obscure yourself from observation. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action to conceal yourself without needing to be {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB} or behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover} or {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}. You need not be out of a creature's line of sight to use this ability.

You become visible at the start of your next turn unless you have moved into a position that allows you to use the {@action Hide|XPHB} action normally. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Long Fade
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on your ability check when you take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action from Fade Away, and you become visible at the end of your next turn instead of the start of your next turn.

entities.name: Fanged Bite
entries: As an {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can make a Bite attack. You are considered proficient with this attack, and you can use Strength or Dexterity as the ability. This attack deals Piercing damage equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier. This Bite attack is not a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

If the attack hits a creature that has blood, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take an additional {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage. You regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Necrotic damage dealt this way. The DC is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.

entities.name: Fey
entries: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the Fey creature type. If you are already proficient in a skill when asked to make an Intelligence or Wisdom ability check relating to Fey, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} instead.
When you gain this feature, you also learn {@language Sylvan}.

entities.name: Fey Form
entries: Your creature type becomes Fey in addition to any other creature type(s) you have.

Also, after you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, pick a damage type from the following list: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Psychic, or Thunder. You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage type until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Fiend
entries: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the Fiend creature type. If you are already proficient in a skill when asked to make an Intelligence or Wisdom ability check relating to Fiends, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} instead.
When you gain this feature, you also learn {@language Abyssal} or {@language Infernal}.

entities.name: Fiend Bound
entries: Your body and soul are bound to the Netherworld. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} as the plane attempts to pull you into it.

entities.name: Fiend Form
entries: Your appearance has grotesquely transformed. Your skin becomes red and leathery, and vicious horns, teeth and nails erupt through the surface of your skin. You can suppress this form and present the appearance of the humanoid you once were, but this is taxing and requires effort. Moments of stress are likely to reveal your true nature. In the following situations your true form may be revealed:

items: Becoming {@condition Bloodied|XPHB}.
{@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on a spell.
Gaining the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition.
Entering hallowed ground.
Entering the presence of Celestials or other Fiends.
Choosing to reveal yourself.

entries: In these events, or times of other extreme emotional or physical stress, a GM can call for a Constitution saving throw with a DC based on your current Transformation Stage. If you fail this save, your Horrific Appearance is revealed.
Non-evil creatures will typically be instantly {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you, unless the GM decides otherwise.

entities.name: Fiendish Soul
entries: You become a Fiend in addition to any other creature type(s) you have. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Deception|XPHB}) checks.
Additionally, choose one of the following damage types and gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to it: Acid, Cold, or Fire.

entities.name: Fighter
entries: You gain proficiency with {@filter martial weapons|items|type=martial weapon} and training with {@item Shield|XPHB|Shields}. In addition, you gain a {@filter Fighting Style feat|feats|category=FS} of your choice.

entities.name: Final Fzeg Bloodline
entries: You reach the full manifestation of your Fzeg Bloodline. You have the following features:

items: Your Strength score increases by 2 points to a maximum of 20.

The damage done by your Claw attack becomes {@dice 3d6} plus your Strength modifier Slashing damage, using your Strength modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} on attack rolls. You can also make three Claw attacks with an {@action Attack|XPHB} action.
You are {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistant} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. Magic and silvered weapons ignore this {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}.
Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet.

entities.name: Final Soman Bloodline
entries: You reach the full manifestation of your Soman Bloodline. You gain the following features:

items: Your Strength and Dexterity scores increase by 1 to a maximum of 20.
{@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24|Fanged Bite's} Necrotic damage increases to {@dice 3d8}.

When a Humanoid creature is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} by your {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} attack or through your feeding, it rises as a {@creature Vampire Spawn|XMM} 24 hours later unless the body is destroyed or undergoes rituals to prevent its rising. You can have two {@creature Vampire Spawn|XMM|Vampire Spawns} created this way under your control, and they follow your verbal commands to the best of their abilities. If you take control of a third {@creature Vampire Spawn|XMM}, you choose one of the other {@creature Vampire Spawn|XMM} you currently control to no longer follow your commands.

entities.name: Final Strigoi Bloodline
entries: You reach the full manifestation of your Strigoi Bloodline. You have the following features:

items: Your Dexterity score increases by 2 points to a maximum of 20.

You can summon {@dice 2d4} {@creature Swarm of Rats|XMM|Swarms of Rats}, {@creature Swarm of Bats|XMM|Swarms of Bats}, or {@creature Wolf|XMM|Wolves} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. They appear at the start of your next turn within 60 feet of you. They follow your verbal or mental commands. They disperse after 1 hour, when you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, or when you dismiss them with a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You regain this ability after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

You can cast the {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} spell up to four times without using a spell slot. You can also cast a spell in this way as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you are targeted with a weapon attack. You cast the spell before taking damage. You regain this ability after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Firm Influence
entries: Others have learned to fear you—and for good reason. You have proficiency in the {@skill Intimidation|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Terrifying Influence
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intimidation checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: First Strike
entries: Hesitation in others is a weakness you've learned to take deadly advantage of. When you hit a creature that hasn't taken a turn in the combat yet, your attack deals an extra {@damage 2d6} damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Strong Strike
entries: If you take this trait twice, you can use the maximum value of the extra damage dice from First Strike, rather than rolling. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Fleet of Foot
entries: As you've learned more than once, moving fast is often the best way to avoid trouble. Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 5 feet.

entries.name: Shared Fleetness
entries: If you take this trait twice, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by another 5 feet, for a total increase of 10 feet.

Additionally, as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose any number of creatures within 30 feet. Those creatures gain a 10 foot bonus to their {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} for 1 minute. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Fleshknit Phosphate
entries: For 1 minute, you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} at the start of each of your turns if you have at least 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}.

entities.name: Flight (Celestial, Dragon, Elemental)
entries: For 10 minutes, you grow feathery, feathery, or spectral wings from your back. You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. You can't benefit from this mutation while wearing Heavy Armor.

entities.name: Focused Mind
entries: Your strength of will protects you from magic that would corrupt your mind. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against being {@condition Charmed|XPHB}.

entries.name: Immutable Mind
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you fail a saving throw against being {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Focused Reserves
entries: No matter how badly beaten down you are, you find the will to keep fighting when you most need it. As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} after you take damage, you can roll a number of d6s equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the total. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Focused Edge
entries: If you take this trait twice, you can reroll 1s and 2s when you use Focused Reserves, but you must use the new rolls.

entities.name: Frayed Thoughts
entries: You suffer the effects of sharing a mind with two personalities. Memories of less practical significance are lost to new ones of midnight hunts. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Intelligence ability checks and saving throws.

entities.name: Fraying Reality
entries: Your mind begins to slip into the dark places where the world of life and world of death intersect. When you become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} for the first time after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you must make a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, you act as though under the effects of a {@spell Confusion|XPHB} spell for 1 minute.

entities.name: Friendless

prerequisite.other: Stage 2 of the {@charoption Shadowsteel Ghoul|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation}

entries: You are no longer able to connect with other creatures on a social level. You prefer isolation and twisted contemplation to friendship and camaraderie.
You cannot be considered an ally to any Humanoids or Beasts, and no living creature can be {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} toward you.
You also have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on all Charisma ability checks.

entities.name: Fzeg Bloodline
entries: Your vampirism follows the Fzeg bloodline, a line of vampires that combines the affliction with lycanthropy. You gain the following features:

items: Your Strength score increases by 2. Your Strength score cannot exceed 20 with this feature.

You gain a Claw attack. This attack deals {@dice 1d8} plus your Strength modifier Slashing damage, using your Strength modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} on attack rolls. This Claw attack is not a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to make the Claw attack, you may use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make a Bite attack from {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24}.
Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet.

entities.name: Ghastly Touch
entries: You can deliver a soul-chilling jolt through your attacks. Once per turn, when you hit a target with a melee attack when you are within 5 feet of the target, you can deal an additional {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Giant
entries: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the Giant creature type. If you are already proficient in a skill when asked to make an Intelligence or Wisdom ability check relating to Giants, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} instead.
When you gain this feature, you also learn {@language Giant}.

entities.name: Gift of Joyous Life
entries: At the beginning of your turn, if you are {@condition Bloodied|XPHB}, you can choose to roll a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} (no action required) and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll. If you roll a 1 on this die, you regain no {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and take 1 point of Force damage instead. You regain this ability when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Gift of Liberating Freedom
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while aren't wearing Heavy armor, you can manifest a pair of leathery wings. You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. These wings recede if you dismiss them as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn, if you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition, or if you become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} while they are manifested.

You can have your wing manifested for 1 hour total, including several shorter stints adding up to 1 hour. You regain your ability to use your wings for an hour after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Gift of Martial Prowess
entries: Once per turn, when you miss with a weapon attack or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can reroll the attack roll. If the reroll hits, you must spend 3 {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} and add the result to the attack's normal damage as Force damage. If the reroll misses, you must spend 3 {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} and take Psychic damage equal to the result. If you have fewer than 3 {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} available, you cannot use this gift.
Once you hit a target with an attack using a rerolled attack, you cannot use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Gift of Prodigious Talent
entries: Choose two Skills. Gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in the chosen skills.

If you roll a 1 or 2 on an {@variantrule Ability Check|XPHB} using one of the chosen skills, you immediately lose half your maximum number of {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, rounded down. You do not regain {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} lost this way until you finish two {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Long Rests}.

entities.name: Gift of Second Chances
entries: When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to roll a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die}. If you roll a 2 or better, set your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} total to the result of the roll. If not, you suffer a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB} failure. You regain the use of this gift when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Gift of Unbridled Power
entries: Upon finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can spend 2 {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} and regain a number of spell slot levels equal to the highest number rolled. However, you take Psychic damage equal to twice the total that you rolled. You regain the reuse of this gift when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Gift of Unconditional Love
entries: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you fails a saving throw, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d10} plus your Transformation Stage. You regain the use of this gift when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
If you roll a 1 on the die, you gain no {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} and instead have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

entities.name: Gift of Unfettered Glory
entries: Each time you damage a creature with a melee attack, an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, or a cantrip, you can do an additional 2 points Force Damage per Transformation Stage.

Each time you roll a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you regain 2 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} fewer per Hit Die.

entities.name: Gift of Unsurpassed Fortune
entries: When an enemy you can see within 10 feet succeeds on an attack roll or a saving throw, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to roll {@dice d20}. On a 6 or higher, the triggering attack or save misses or fails, and you regain the use of your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}. Otherwise, you take {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage per Transformation Stage, which cannot be reduced. You regain your use of this ability when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Gifted Performer
entries: When you desire to stand out, you have a natural gift for impressing others. You have proficiency in the {@skill Performance|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: A Sight to Behold
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Performance checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Grandmother's Curse
entries: You learn one Shadowsteel curse of your choice. You always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day. You can cast it once per day without using a spell slot or needing spell components.

entities.name: Grave-Touched Soul
entries: You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage. If you already have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage, you gain {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} instead.

entities.name: Greater Magic Tricks
entries: Your Fey masters provide even further magical abilities. You gain the ability to cast one level 3 spell, one level 4 spell, and one level 5 spell. You can cast each of these once without using a spell slot or needing spell components. You regain the ability to cast these spells when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. The DC for these spells is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.

entries.name: Spring
entries: Your spells are {@spell Stinking Cloud|XPHB}, {@spell Vitriolic Sphere|XPHB}, and {@spell Cloudkill|XPHB}.

entries.name: Summer
entries: Your spells are {@spell Fireball|XPHB}, {@spell Fire Shield|XPHB}, and {@spell Dream|XPHB}.

entries.name: Autumn
entries: Your spells are {@spell Lightning Bolt|XPHB}, {@spell Blight|XPHB}, and {@spell Hold Monster|XPHB}.

entries.name: Winter
entries: Your spells are {@spell Vampiric Touch|XPHB}, {@spell Ice Storm|XPHB}, and {@spell Cone of Cold|XPHB}.

entities.name: Greater Sanguine Curse
entries: Your curse has taken a stronger hold onto you. You gain the following features:

items: You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on all {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests} when you are in sunlight.
You must feed every 4 days.

entities.name: Hag Form
entries: Your {@variantrule Creature Type|XPHB} becomes Fey in addition to any other creature type(s) you have.

Additionally, your connection with the magic of the natural world provides you with increased defenses. When you are not wearing armor, your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} is 13 plus your Dexterity modifier. Additionally, choose Strength, Intelligence, or Charisma: you are proficient with Saving Throws using that ability score.

entities.name: Hardened Defense (Armor Modification)
entries: Hardened scales or magical pelts make your armor difficult to pierce. While wearing your crafted armor, you gain a +2 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}.

entities.name: Haunting Flight
entries: You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

Also, while you are flying, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to focus your terrifying presence on one creature you can see within 30 feet. That creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The DC for the Wisdom saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage. Once you use this feature, you must finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can use it again.

entities.name: Healing Resistance

prerequisite.other: Stage 3 of the {@charoption Shadowsteel Ghoul|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation}

entries: You heal at a slower rate than normal, whether that healing is magical or natural. Whenever you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you regain half as many {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} as you should. You still recover all {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the end of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Helping Hand
entries: You excel at aiding your allies, knowing that the time will come when you need them to return the favor. You can use the {@action Help|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to assist any ally making an ability check. (This is an Exploration trait.)

entries.name: Helpful Tactics
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you use Helping Hand, you can also assist an ally making an attack roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. (This is a Combat trait.)

entities.name: Hideous Appearance (Aberrant Horror)

prerequisite.other: Stage 2 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}

entries: Your appearance has grotesquely transformed. You may have turned into a hulking mass of flesh adorned with countless eyes, or a bone-and-tusk-covered behemoth. Regardless of your true form, you're horrific to behold.

You can suspend this form and take on the appearance of the humanoid you once were, but this is taxing and requires effort. This form is not permanent, and moments of stress are likely to reveal your true nature. Your true form may be revealed in the following situations:

items: Becoming {@condition Bloodied|XPHB}
{@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on a spell
Gaining the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition
Entering hallowed ground
Choosing to reveal yourself

entries: In these events, or times of other extreme emotional or physical stress, a GM can call or a Constitution saving throw with a DC based on your current Transformation Stage. If you fail this save, your Horrific Appearance is revealed.
Non-evil creatures that witness your true form become instantly {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you, unless the GM decides otherwise.

entities.name: Hideous Appearance (Hag)
entries: Your appearance is grotesquely transformed. Your body, hair, and eyes change in ways mortals find abhorrent. Most creatures witnessing your true form become {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} and no one is likely to trust you, unless the GM decides otherwise. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) checks.

You cannot stand to look upon your own reflection. The first time you see your reflection after a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you must succeed on a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw or gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.
Your appearance changes based on your Stage 1 Sisterhood Boon:

entries.name: Green Sisterhood
entries: You wither or bloat as your body struggles to deal with the ravages of age. Your hair becomes white and several feet in length. Your skin becomes green and covered in warts and boils.

entries.name: Red Sisterhood
entries: Your eyes have turned entirely red, and your pupils are narrow like a cat's. Your skin becomes blood red.

entries.name: Sea Sisterhood
entries: You have slimy scales and the pallid skin of a dead fish that sags from your emaciated body. Your hair resembles seaweed, and your eyes are glassy.

entities.name: Hideous Appearance (Lich)
entries: Your appearance has grotesquely transformed. Your flesh withers and contracts around your bones, your eyes become sunken, and you reek of death. Regardless of your true form, you're horrific to behold.

You can suspend this form and manifest the appearance of the humanoid you once were, but this is taxing and requires effort. This form is not permanent, and moments of stress are likely to reveal your true nature. Your true form may be revealed in the following situations:

items: Becoming {@condition Bloodied|XPHB}
{@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on a spell
Having the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition
Entering hallowed ground
Choosing to reveal yourself

entries: In these events, or times of other extreme emotional or physical stress, a GM can call or a Constitution saving throw with a DC based on your current Transformation Stage. If you fail this save, your Horrific Appearance is revealed.
Non-evil creatures that witness your true form become instantly {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you, unless the GM decides otherwise.

entities.name: Hold Breath
entries: Whether trapped under black water or resisting poisonous fumes, you refuse to give in. You can hold your breath for up to 1 hour.

entries.name: Endless Breath
entries: If you take this trait twice, you can hold your breath for up to 8 hours.

entities.name: Holy Strikes
entries: When you hit with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} or damage a creature with a cantrip, you can add {@damage 1d6} Radiant damage to that attack. You can add this damage a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

At higher Transformation Stages, you deal an additional {@damage 1d6} Radiant damage per Transformation Stage, for a total of {@dice 2d6} at Stage 2, {@dice 3d6} at Stage 3, and {@dice 4d6} at Stage 4.

entities.name: Hunter's Focus
entries: While in hybrid form, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to mark one creature within 60 feet as your prey. A creature remains marked this way for 1 hour, or until it dies. You may only have 1 creature marked at a time.
While a creature is marked as your prey, you gain the following benefits:

items: Whenever you hit the marked creature with a melee attack, you deal an additional {@damage 1d6} damage of that attack's type.
You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB} or {@skill Survival|XPHB}) check you make to find it.

entities.name: Hunter's Instinct
entries: You summon a surge of ferocity when your prey least expects it. At the end of each {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you gain a number of d8s equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. When you make an attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can roll a {@dice d8} and add it to either the attack roll or the damage roll. If you add it to the {@dice d20} roll, you can decide to roll the {@dice d8} after the {@dice d20} roll is made, but you must do so before the outcome of the roll is known.

entries.name: Relentless Instinct
entries: If you take this trait twice, whenever you use Hunter's Instinct for an attack roll, if the attack roll misses, you retain the {@dice d8} and can use it again.

entities.name: Hybrid Bear Form
entries: You become a Monstrosity in addition to any other creature type(s).
As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you may voluntarily transform into a hybrid half-bear creature. While in your hybrid bear form, the following rules apply:

items: Your Strength score becomes 20 unless it was higher.
You gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
You can't cast spells or concentrate on spells. Your ability to speak is reduced to short, basic, guttural responses.
While transformed, you can use weapons and equipment as normal, unless specified elsewhere.

You gain a Claw attack that you may use in place of any weapon attack. This attack deals {@dice 1d8} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

Immediately after hitting a target with a Claw attack, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to grab the creature with that hand, as long as it is no more than one size category larger than you. The grabbed target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition. The escape DC is 8 plus your Strength modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

entries: Your hybrid bear form lasts 1 hour per Transformation Stage. If you entered your hybrid form voluntarily, you can revert to your normal form by using a {@action Magic|XPHB} action on your turn. ({@filter Flaws listed below|optionalfeatures|source=grimhollowpg24|feature type=ly:tf|miscellaneous=} may override this rule.)

entities.name: Hybrid Form Affinity
entries: You have achieved a state of equilibrium that most Lycanthropes never find. You gain the following benefits while you are voluntarily in your hybrid form:

items: You can speak normally.

You can cast spells without needing to provide their Verbal or Somatic components. Additionally, you can cast spells without needing to provide material components, provided they do not have a GP value listed.
Allies within 20 feet of you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ability checks and saving throws.

entities.name: Hybrid Rat Form
entries: You become a Monstrosity in addition to any other creature type(s).
As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you may voluntarily transform into a hybrid half-rat creature. While in your hybrid rat form, the following rules apply:

items: Your Dexterity score becomes 18 unless it was higher.
You can {@action Hide|XPHB} or {@action Disengage|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
You gain proficiency with {@skill Stealth|XPHB}, and you can double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} when making a Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) check.
You can't cast spells or concentrate on spells. Your ability to speak is reduced to short, basic, guttural responses.
While transformed, you can use weapons and equipment as normal, unless specified elsewhere.

You gain a Claw or Bite attack that you may use in place of any weapon attack. This attack deals {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
After taking an {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you may take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} later in that turn to attack once with either your Claw or Bite.
If you target a creature that is within 5 feet of one of its enemies, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Claw or Bite attacks made against the target.

entries: Your hybrid rat form lasts 1 hour per Transformation Stage. If you entered your hybrid form voluntarily, you can revert to your normal form by using a {@action Magic|XPHB} action on your turn. ({@filter Flaws listed below|optionalfeatures|source=grimhollowpg24|feature type=ly:tf|miscellaneous=} may override this rule.)

entities.name: Hybrid Wolf Form
entries: You become a Monstrosity in addition to any other creature type(s).
As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you may voluntarily transform into a hybrid half-wolf creature. While in your hybrid wolf form, the following rules apply:

items: Your Strength score becomes 18 unless it is higher.
Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet, and you can use the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
You can't cast spells or concentrate on spells. Your ability to speak is reduced to short, basic, guttural responses.
While transformed, you can use weapons and equipment as normal, unless specified elsewhere.

You gain a Claw or Bite attack that you may use in place of any weapon attack. These attacks deal {@dice 1d8} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
After taking an {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you may take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} later in that turn to attack once with either your Claw or Bite.

entries: Your hybrid wolf form lasts 1 hour per Transformation Stage. If you entered your hybrid form voluntarily, you can revert to your normal form by using a {@action Magic|XPHB} action on your turn. ({@filter Flaws listed below|optionalfeatures|source=grimhollowpg24|feature type=ly:tf|miscellaneous=} may override this rule.)

entities.name: Illusionary Cloak
entries: You can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to wrap yourself in an illusionary cloak for up to 1 hour. During this time, you can make yourself look like another creature of your general size and humanoid shape. This illusion ends when you use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to end it, or if you die or gain the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition.
A creature can pierce the illusion by taking the {@action Study|XPHB} action and succeeding on a DC 20 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check, thus seeing your true form.

entities.name: Impromptu Artisan
entries: You've never known the luxury of always having the  gear you need, but you have more than learned to make do. If you possess {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} with which you have proficiency, and if you have access to appropriate raw materials and any additional necessary equipment (as the GM determines), you can use a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} to craft any one nonmagical item worth 10 GP or less, including:

items: {@filter Adventuring gear|items|type=adventuring gear}
{@filter A weapon or shield|items|type=simple weapon;martial weapon;shield|Cost=[&0;&1000]|miscellaneous=mundane;!reprinted}
A unique item that performs a simple function approved by the GM

entries: The gear you create is workable but not high quality, and can't be sold except as the GM determines.

entries.name: Master Artisan
entries: If you take this trait twice, you can use Impromptu Artisan during a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, during which you craft one nonmagical item worth 50 GP or less.

entities.name: Improved Fanged Bite
entries: As an {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can make 2 {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} attacks. Additionally, you can make a {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} attack as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} if you don't make a {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} attack as part of an {@action Attack|XPHB} action on the same turn.

entities.name: Improviser
entries: When needs demand, you get the job done better than most. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose one skill or tool that you don't have proficiency with. You have proficiency in that skill or with that tool for 1 hour. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Expert Improviser
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks you make using the skill or tool you select with Improviser. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Inborn Perception
entries: The best way to avoid danger is to make sure you're the first person to notice it. You have proficiency in the {@skill Perception|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Piercing Perception
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Perception checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: You are able to loosen the binds that tie you to the material world to move through solid objects and creatures. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can make yourself incorporeal until the start of your next turn. While in this form, you gain the following benefits:

items: You can move through solid objects at your full {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. You take {@damage 1d10} Force damage if you end your turn inside a solid object and are moved to the nearest open space.
You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all damage types except Force damage.
You are {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB}.

entries: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Infernal Smite
entries: Once on each of your turns, you can choose one creature you have just damaged with a weapon attack, an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, or a cantrip. Deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Acid, Cold, or Fire damage to that creature, using the same damage type you chose for {@optfeature Fiendish Soul|GrimHollowPG24}. You can add this damage a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage, but no more than once per damage roll. You regain all uses of this ability when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
At higher Transformation Stages, you do an additional {@damage 1d6} of damage per Transformation Stage, for a total of {@dice 2d6} at Stage 2, {@dice 3d6} at Stage 3, and {@dice 4d6} at Stage 4.

entities.name: Infernal Summons
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can tear open a portal to the Netherworld and summon up to four {@filter Fiends of CR 4|bestiary|source=|Challenge Rating=[&0;&4]|type=fiend} or lower within 30 feet of you. The Fiends remain for 1 minute (or less, if the GM decides what Fiends answer the summons).

The summoned Fiends are {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} to you and your companions. They act directly after you in the initiative order. They obey any verbal commands that you issue to them (no action required by you). If you don't issue any commands to them, they use the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action on their turn and take no other movement, {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB|Bonus Actions}, or {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} unless it is to protect themselves.

entities.name: Infused (Dragon, Elemental, Ooze)
entries: Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder. For 10 minutes, whenever you hit with an attack, you can cause it to deal the chosen type rather than its normal damage type.

entities.name: Inhuman Reflexes
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws.

entities.name: Instinctive Stealth
entries: When trouble comes for you, you excel at making sure it can't find you. You have proficiency in the {@skill Stealth|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Calculated Disappearance
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Stealth|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Instrumentalist
entries: In the quieter moments, music can help you forget the horrors you've seen. You have proficiency with {@item Musical Instrument|XPHB|two instruments} of your choice.

entries.name: Virtuoso
entries: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain proficiency with two new instruments each time.

Additionally, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks made using any instrument. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Intrinsic Orientation
entries: A single misstep can lead to ruin, but your instincts for direction keep you from going astray. You always know which way is north, and you can reckon a cardinal direction of the GM's determination while on other planes. Additionally, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks made to avoid becoming lost, to navigate, or to track.

entries.name: Expert Orientation
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you fail an ability check made to avoid becoming lost, to navigate, or to track, you can choose to succeed instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Intuitive Acrobat
entries: Staying loose and limber means being able to get out of even the tightest spots when your life is on the line. You have proficiency in the {@skill Acrobatics|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Stunt Expert
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Acrobatics|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Inured to the Elements
entries: Even beneath scorching sun and in freezing cold, you hold yourself strong. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws made to resist the effects of extreme cold or extreme heat.

entries.name: Immune to the Elements
entries: If you take this trait twice, you automatically succeed on Constitution saving throws to resist the effects of extreme cold or extreme heat.

entities.name: Invisibility (Fey, Fiend, Undead)
entries: For 10 minutes, you have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition. This effect ends early if you dismiss it (no action required), or you attack or cast a spell.

entities.name: Iron Pelt
entries: While in hybrid form, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. Magical and silvered weapons ignore this {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}.

entities.name: Iron Sensitivity
entries: Your body and soul connect back to the Realm of Faerie, and iron and steel weapons forged of the mortal realm cause you pain. After each {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, the first time you are damaged by an iron or steel weapon, you must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.

entities.name: Ironmind Injection
entries: For 1 hour, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

entities.name: Irrepressible Sight
entries: Any foe you can see is a foe you can take down—so you make sure nothing prevents you from seeing. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against having the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Resolute Sight
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you fail a saving throw against having the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature after you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Keen Survivor
entries: The wilds of Etharis have claimed many who lack the skill to navigate them. You have proficiency in the {@skill Survival|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Determined Survivor
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Survival|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Kindred Form
entries: You gain the ability to transform into the animal form representative of your Lycanthropy type, known as your Kindred Form. Use the stat block of the creature your lycanthropy corresponds with and follow the rules of the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell:

You are indistinguishable from other creatures of that type, and you can communicate with those creatures. You cannot speak, but you can understand languages you know. All equipment you wear and carry falls off you when transforming.

entities.name: Larger Target
entries: Foes that outsize you quickly learn to fear your wrath. If you hit a creature that is one size larger than you, you can choose to deal extra damage to the creature equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Even Larger
entries: If you take this trait twice, Larger Target applies to creatures of any size larger than you.

entities.name: Legion of Slime
entries: When you manifest your Ooze Form, it lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

While you are manifesting your Ooze Form and you are hit by an attack that causes you to become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB}, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to split into two identical Oozes. Each Ooze uses your game statistics, except that they are one size category smaller than your normal size, and your remaining {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} Maximum are divided evenly between the two Oozes (round down). Your equipment is not duplicated; all of it remains on one of the Oozes.

Each Ooze acts on your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}. They can each move independently, but they can only collectively take one action and {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn and one {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} per round.
After you use this feature, you cannot use it again until after you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Lich Magica
entries: You can trade the soul in your soul vessel for increased magical power.
On your turn, as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can spend the charge in your soul vessel to gain one of the following effects:

items: Regain a spell slot equal to or lower than your current Lich Transformation Stage.
The next saving throw made before the end of your next turn against one of your spells has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.
The next time you cast a spell before the end of your next turn, deal an extra {@dice 1d6} Necrotic damage per Lich Transformation Stage to one creature damaged by that spell.

The next time you cast a spell before the end of your next turn that restores {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, that spell restores an additional {@dice 1d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} per Lich Transformation Stage.

entities.name: Light Armor Training
entries: Dealing with the threats you face requires the right combination of protection and movement. You have training with Light armor.

entries.name: Light Armor Expertise
entries: If you take this trait twice, your AC increases by 1 while wearing Light armor.

entities.name: Liquid Courage
entries: You gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to twice your Fighter level plus your Constitution modifier. If any of these {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} remain after 1 minute, they vanish.

entities.name: Liquid Rage
entries: For 1 minute, your attacks with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} deal an extra {@damage 1d4} damage on hit.

entities.name: Lord of Undeath
entries: When you use {@optfeature Acolyte of Undeath|GrimHollowPG24} you can choose to transform the target into a {@creature Ghoul|XMM} instead of a {@creature Zombie|XMM}.

entities.name: Lucky
entries: The luck you carry will see you through the worst Etharis has to offer. When you roll a 1 on a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can reroll that die but must use the new roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Master of Fortune
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the reroll made with Lucky.

entities.name: Lust for the Hunt
entries: The savage nature of your curse sometimes causes you to lose control. These ferocious tendencies are a constant struggle between you and the beast within.

The first time you become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you must succeed on a DC 10 Wisdom saving throw. If you are in the light of a full moon, you automatically fail this saving throw.
If you fail this saving throw, you are subjected to the following rules until you can end the hybrid form:

items: You immediately enter your hybrid form. You go completely feral, and you cannot use any of your equipment or class features.

On your turn while in hybrid form, you must move toward the closest creature you can see, smell, or hear, prioritizing {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} creatures. If you end your movement and no creature is within 5 feet of you, you must use the {@action Dash|XPHB} action to move toward the closest one.
If there is a creature within 5 feet of you and you have not used your action, you must make an {@action Attack|XPHB} action using your Claw attack.
When you damage a creature on your turn and it is not reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you must use further attacks that turn against that creature.

entries: If you are at full {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of your turn, you can make a DC 10 Wisdom saving throw. On a success, you return to your normal form. You also return to your normal form if {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} is cast on you, or after 1 hour passes.

entities.name: Magic Resistance

prerequisite.other: Stage 2 of the {@charoption Shadowsteel Ghoul|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation}

entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells.

entities.name: Magic Tricks
entries: Your Fey masters provide you with the ability to weave magic based on your Fey Court allegiance. You gain the ability to cast one cantrip, one level 1 spell, and one level 2 spell. You can cast each of these once without using a spell slot or needing spell components. You regain the ability to cast these spells when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. The DC for these spells is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.

entries.name: Spring
entries: Your cantrip is {@spell Poison Spray|XPHB}, and your spells are {@spell Fog Cloud|XPHB} and {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}.

entries.name: Summer
entries: Your cantrip is {@spell Fire Bolt|XPHB}, and your spells are {@spell Burning Hands|XPHB} and {@spell Flame Blade|XPHB}.

entries.name: Autumn
entries: Your cantrip is {@spell Shillelagh|XPHB}, and your spells are {@spell Thunderwave|XPHB} and {@spell Ray of Enfeeblement|XPHB}.

entries.name: Winter
entries: Your cantrip is {@spell Ray of Frost|XPHB}, and your spells are {@spell Ice Knife|XPHB} and {@spell Darkness|XPHB}.

entities.name: Magical Fortification
entries: The more that magic threatens you, the more your resilience to it increases. Choose an ability score: Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws using that ability score against spells and other magical effects.

entries.name: Extended Fortification
entries: If you take this trait multiple times, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws using a new ability score each time.

Additionally, if you fail a saving throw against a spell or other magical effect and you do not have proficiency with that saving throw, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reroll the save. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Magical Insight
entries: Magic is power in the right hands, and those hands are yours. You have proficiency in the {@skill Arcana|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Magical Historian
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Arcana|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Magical Savvy
entries: Whether through intensive study or the innate touch of magic in your blood, you have the ability to invoke magical spells. You learn one cantrip of your choice from any spell list, which you cast using the associated ability score: Intelligence for Wizard spells, Wisdom for Cleric and Druid spells, and Charisma for Bard, Sorcerer, and Warlock spells. If the spell appears on multiple spell lists, choose one to determine the spellcasting attribute for that spell.

entries.name: Magical Savant
entries: If you take this trait multiple times, you select a different cantrip each time, or you can select a level 1 spell from the same list as a cantrip you have previously chosen. If you select a level 1 spell, you can cast it once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. If you have levels in the associated spellcasting class, you always have this spell prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day.

entities.name: Manipulate Objects
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 1 Mutation Point}
As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can take the {@action Utilize|XPHB} action.

entities.name: Master of Distraction
entries: You draw your foes' attention to you, intending it to be the last diversion they ever see. As an {@action Influence|XPHB} action, you put on a tactical display (bravado, cowardice, confusion, or some other tactic) that gets your enemies' attention. Until the end of your next turn, any attack on an enemy within 10 feet of you that could see you when you took the {@action Influence|XPHB} action, is made with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Hindering Distraction
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you use Master of Distraction, one affected enemy of your choice also has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls it makes against any of your allies until the end of your next turn.

entities.name: Master of Many
entries: You add a third elemental to your {@optfeature Elemental Affinity|GrimHollowPG24|Elemental Affinity Boon}. When you do so, you gain the associated benefits of your new element.

entities.name: Master of the Green Sisterhood
entries: You come into your power as a Hag and enhance your abilities.

items: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you may make an additional Claw attack. Your Claw attacks deal an additional {@damage 1d6} Poison damage.

You learn a corrupt ritual to create a Hag Eye or a {@creature Gasdra|GrimHollowPG24} companion. The ritual takes four hours and 200 GP to complete. You choose which ritual you know when you select this boon. You can only have one Hag Eye or Gasdra companion at a time, and you cannot have both.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can recover an expended spell slot by spending {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} equal to the spell slot's level. The spell slot can have a level equal to no more than one third your character level (round up). Once you use this boon, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Master of the Red Sisterhood
entries: You come into your power as a Hag and enhance your abilities.

items: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you may make an additional Claw attack. Your Claw attacks deal an additional {@damage 1d4} Psychic damage. You regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Psychic damage dealt.

Your banter becomes supernaturally charming. A creature under your magical influence (such as being {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by you) who is able to hear you for 1 minute must must make a Charisma saving throw with a DC equal to 14 plus your Transformation Stage. On a failed save, when your mental influence ends, the target forgets magic was used to influence it. You regain use of this feature after you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} to cast the {@spell Charm Person|XPHB} spell.

entities.name: Master of the Sea Sisterhood
entries: You come into your power as a Hag and enhance your abilities.

items: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you may make an additional Claw attack. Your Claw attacks deal an additional {@damage 1d6} Slashing damage.

Your appearance becomes so vile that it terrifies mortal creatures. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gaze upon a Beast or Humanoid within 30 feet that can see your true form. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to 14 plus your Charisma modifier. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the start of its next turn. A target that succeeds is immune to this feature for 24 hours. You regain use of this feature after you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend and roll a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} to touch a creature and allow the target to breathe water for a number of minutes equal to the amount rolled.

entities.name: Master of Undeath
entries: Undead creatures under your control from a Lich Transformation Boon have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls when they are within 5 feet of you.

Additionally, whenever an Undead creature you control due to a Lich Transformation Boon is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can spend the charge of your soul vessel as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}. If you do, that Undead creature is at 1 hit point instead and may immediately move up to its full speed and make an {@action Attack|XPHB} action with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. This feature has no effect if the creature has been reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} by Radiant damage.

entities.name: Masterful Aptitude
entries: Your discipline and focus give you an edge that others lack. Choose one of your skill or tool proficiencies. You have {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} on ability checks made using the chosen proficiency.

entries.name: Focused Mastery
entries: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain its benefit for a new skill proficiency or tool proficiency each time.

Additionally, when you make a check using a skill or tool for which you've taken Masterful Aptitude, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Meditative Rest
entries: Sleep is a luxury you've never needed to afford. When you rest, you meditate deeply for 4 hours, dreaming but remaining conscious. After resting in this way, you gain the same benefit that other humanoids do from 8 hours of sleep.

entries.name: Restorative Rest
entries: If you take this trait twice, you need to spend only 2 hours in your meditation to gain the benefit of 8 hours of sleep, and you gain a {@dice d6} at the end of each {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Before the end of your next {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can roll the {@dice d6} and add it to any {@dice d20} Test you make. You can decide to roll the {@dice d6} after the {@dice d20} Test is made, but you must do so before the outcome of the roll is known.

entities.name: Medium Armor Training
entries: The pounding you routinely take in combat requires a formidable layer of defense. You have training with Medium armor and with Shields.

entries.name: Heavy Armor Training
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have training with Heavy armor.

entities.name: Melted Appearance
entries: Your body is now a hideous lump of dripping flesh excreting foul smelling slime. While your form remains roughly humanoid, the horror of what you are is clear to anyone that looks upon your molten face. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} when you use the {@action Influence|XPHB} action to make Charisma-based ability checks made against creatures that are not Aberrations or Oozes.

In addition, your Transformation to an Ooze has liquefied many of your organs and dimmed your senses. Your eyes melt and run down your face and there is nothing but flesh where your ears once were. You permanently have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition beyond the range of your {@sense Blindsight|XPHB}.

entities.name: Memori Lichdom
entries: While your soul vessel is charged, you gain the following benefits to your weapon attacks or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}:

items: They count as magical.
They have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} if taken as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

After you deal damage with a weapon attack or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can spend a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} later on that turn and expend one of your {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}. Roll the die and deal Necrotic damage equal to the result to one target damaged by the triggering attack or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

entities.name: Menacing Roar
entries: Your battle cry can cause even the most formidable foes to quail before you. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you emit a roar, shout, or other loud vocal outburst. Each creature of your choice within 10 feet of you that can hear you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} + your Constitution modifier) or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condtion until the end of your next turn. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Incomparable Roar
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you use Menacing Roar, one target of your choice has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw.

entities.name: Might (Beast, Construct, Giant)
entries: For 1 minute, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and saving throws. You also have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls using Strength, and you deal {@damage 1d6} extra damage to targets you hit with a Strength-based attack. The damage is the same type dealt by the attack.

entities.name: Mighty Shove
entries: Your powerful blows send your targets reeling. When you hit a creature no more than one size larger than you with a melee attack, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to attempt to shove that creature. The target must succeed on a Strength or Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your Strength modifier + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or be pushed up to 10 feet away from you.

entries.name: Overwhelming Shove
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you use Mighty Shove, the target creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw.

entities.name: Mimic Object
entries: When you manifest your Ooze Form, it lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

While manifesting your Ooze Form, you can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to transform into an object of your size or up to two sizes smaller. Any equipment worn or carried is dropped in your space. While mimicking an object, you retain your statistics.

While mimicking an object, you are indistinguishable from the object and you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you are touched to adhere to the creature that touches you. The creature has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition. The DC to escape is equal to DC equal to 8 plus your Constitution modifier plus your Transformation Stage. Ability checks made to escape this grapple have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

entities.name: Mindful Investigator
entries: Putting together the pieces of even the darkest mysteries is second nature to you. You have proficiency in the {@skill Investigation|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Thorough Sleuth
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Investigation|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Mist Form
entries: You can cast the {@spell Gaseous Form|XPHB} spell a number of times equal to your Vampire Transformation Stage without using a spell slot or needing to use Verbal, Somatic, or Material components. You regain all uses of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

You can cast this spell as an {@variantrule Action|XPHB} on your turn, or as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you would take Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage. You cast the spell before taking the damage.

entities.name: Monk
entries: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}, your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers. You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}, and you can roll {@dice 1d6} in place of the normal damage of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. In addition, after you cast a spell that has a casting time of an action or take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can make one attack with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entities.name: Monstrosity
entries: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the Monstrosity creature type. If you are already proficient in a skill when asked to make an Intelligence or Wisdom ability check relating to Monstrosities, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} instead.
When you gain this feature, you also learn {@language Deep Speech}.

entities.name: Moved by Faith
entries: The grimmest myths and legends of the past hold the keys to shaping the future. You have proficiency in the {@skill Religion|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Force of Faith
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Religion|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Multiattack
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 2 Mutation Points}
You can attack twice with a Wild Shape form's attack instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

entities.name: Mutable Corpus
entries: You have abundant control over your Ooze Form. Each time you manifest your Ooze Form, choose one of the following augmentations:

items: {@i Breathless}. You do not need to breathe.

{@i Disguise}. You remold your features to look different. You can change your apparent species, seem 1 foot shorter or taller, and appear heavier or lighter (you must keep the same basic arrangement of limbs). To discern the disguise, a creature must take the {@action Study|XPHB} action and succeed on an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check against DC 10 + your Constitution modifier + your Transformation Stage.
{@i Transparent}. Your body becomes transparent. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks.

entities.name: Mystic Monster
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 3 Mutation Points}

You can cast spells while you're in this Wild Shape form. If you have 18 or more Druid levels, creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells you cast while this Mutation is active.

entities.name: Natural Armor
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 2 Mutation Points}
You gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}.

entries.name: Repeatable
entries: You can gain this Mutation more than once, but no more than three times.

entities.name: Natural Attack
entries: The gift of natural weaponry means you are never unarmed, as your foes learn to their peril. Your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} deal damage equal to {@dice 1d6} + your Strength or Dexterity modifier. The type of damage dealt by your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} can be Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing, based on the type of natural weaponry you possess (claws, horns, a tail, and so forth).

entries.name: Swift Strike
entries: If you take this trait twice, you can use {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Natural Camouflage
entries: Your ability to fade into the background of familiar territory helps keep you safe from threats. Choose an environment:  arctic, coastal, desert, forest, grassland, hill and mountain, swamp, subterranean, or underwater. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Stealth checks made with the {@action Hide|XPHB} action while in that environment.

entries.name: Shared Camouflage
entries: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain its benefits for a new environment each time.

Additionally, when you take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action, you can forgo making a {@skill Stealth|XPHB} check while in any environment chosen with Natural Camouflage, instead treating the check as if you had rolled a 15. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Natural Movement
entries: The time you've spent in the natural world lets you travel at speed, and hinders the abilities of those who would hunt you. Choose an environment:  arctic, coastal, desert, forest, grassland, hill and mountain, swamp, subterranean, or underwater. While in that environment, moving through nonmagical {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} costs you no extra movement, and ability checks made to track you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

entries.name: Shared Movement
entries: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain its benefits for a new environment each time. Additionally, while in any environment chosen for Natural Movement, as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can grant creatures of your choice the benefit of Natural Movement for 1 hour, as long as those creatures remain within 120 feet of you and can see you.

entities.name: Natural Weapons (Beast, Dragon, Monstrosity)
entries: For 1 hour, you gain natural weapons. You can roll {@dice 1d6} in place of the normal damage of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. This damage can be Piercing or Slashing. You choose each time you make an attack. You can also make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls of your Unarmed Strikes.

entities.name: Nature's Voice
entries: Mastering the subtle expression of fauna and flora grants you an edge in dealing with the threats of the wilderness. Through sounds and gestures, you can communicate simple ideas with Beasts and Plant creatures, understanding if a creature is hungry, for example. This gives you no specific ability to control such creatures, and you can't understand or learn detailed information from them.

entries.name: Primal Voice
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks made as part of an {@action Influence|XPHB} action to interact with a Beast or Plant creature.

entities.name: Necromantic Dystrophia
entries: When your soul vessel is not charged, you suffer the following effects:

items: You cannot use the {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Dodge|XPHB}, or {@action Disengage|XPHB} actions.
You cannot use {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions}.
You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws.
You cannot disguise your {@optfeature Hideous Appearance (Lich)|GrimHollowPG24|Hideous Appearance}.

entries: Upon charging your soul vessel with an appropriate soul, you are no longer subject to these effects.

entities.name: Nimble Moves
entries: You have learned the value of being able to manipulate the world around you without attracting the notice of others. You have proficiency in the {@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Exquisite Legerdemain
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Ooze
entries: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the Ooze creature type. If you are already proficient in a skill when asked to make an Intelligence or Wisdom ability check relating to Oozes, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} instead.
When you gain this feature, you also learn {@language Deep Speech} or {@language Undercommon}.

entities.name: Ooze Form
entries: You become an Ooze in addition to any other creature type(s) you are. You gain {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet. If you already have {@sense Blindsight|XPHB}, your range increases by 30 feet.
