You can will your body to melt and flow, becoming more fluid than solid. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you manifest your Ooze Form. You become amorphous, able to move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement, and you are {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions. You remain in Ooze Form for 1 minute or until you use your {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to return to normal.

You can manifest your Ooze Form a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation stage. You regain all uses of this feature after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Out of Phase
entries: Your corporeal presence shifts and fades, softening your enemies' ability to harm you. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, for 1 minute, all creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against you, and you can move through other creature's spaces without treating them as {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Phase Shift
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you use Out of Phase, you can extend its benefit to any ally within 10 feet of you.

entities.name: Overwhelming Brand
entries: You can apply one of the following additional effects to a creature under the effect of your {@optfeature Daemonic Brand|GrimHollowPG24}.

items: The creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved, and you can move the creature up to 10 feet horizontally on your turn (no action required).
The creature has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition. On your turn, you can choose to remove this condition (no action required).

entities.name: Pack Hunter
entries: Fighting in the thick of battle lets you aid your allies when it counts. When an ally within 10 feet of you is about to make an attack roll or a saving throw, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to grant that ally {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack or save. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Pack Leader
entries: If you take this trait twice, Pack Hunter can be triggered by any ally within 30 feet of you. Additionally, if the attack roll misses or the saving throw fails, you don't lose that usage of Pack Hunter.

entities.name: Pack Tactics
entries: Staying close to your allies in combat makes you even more dangerous. When you start your turn with at least one ally who isn't {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} within 5 feet of another creature you can see, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against that creature until the end of your turn.

entries.name: Pack Instinct
entries: If you take this trait twice, gaining {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} from Pack Tactics requires no action.

entities.name: Paladin
entries: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a melee attack using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend a spell slot to deal an extra {@dice 1d6} Radiant damage to the target, plus another {@dice 1d6} per level of the spell slot.
The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} if the target is a Fiend or an Undead.
Once you use this talent, you must finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can use it again.

entities.name: Paralyzing Touch
entries: Your Ghastly Touch feature takes on a spirit-freezing quality. The damage from your {@optfeature Ghastly Touch|GrimHollowPG24} increases to {@damage 2d6} Necrotic damage. Additionally, you can force any creature you damage with {@optfeature Ghastly Touch|GrimHollowPG24} to make a Constitution saving throw. The DC for this saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.

On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, they have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition instead. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Pass Through
entries: Making use of constant movement lets you minimize the threat of larger foes. You can move through the space of any creature at least on size larger than you.

entries.name: Nimble Passage
entries: If you take this trait twice, you do not treat another creature's space as {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.

entities.name: Personal Bastion
entries: Focusing all your resolve, you stand fast and watch your enemies flail against your defenses. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you become motionless and gain the following effects:

items: You have a +4 bonus to AC.
You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws.
You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength and Constitution saving throws.
Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is 0 and you can't benefit from any bonus to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
You lose {@status Concentration|XPHB}.

entries: You can't take actions, and you can't use your {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} except to end the effect of this trait.

entries.name: Mobile Bastion
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you use Personal Bastion, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to half your normal {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} (rounded down), you do not have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws, and you can use {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB|Bonus Actions}.

entities.name: Persuasive Knack
entries: You have learned that the best way to deal with certain threats is to keep those threats from escalating. You have proficiency in the {@skill Persuasion|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Tongue of Gold
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Persuasion|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Phase Leap (Armor Modification)
entries: You have powdered your armor with fey dust or sewn a pelt of a phase-shifting monstrosity into it. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

entities.name: Physical Deterioration
entries: Your skin sloughs away in thick, syrupy sheets and your bones become like melted wax. The sun itself burns you, and holy light becomes lethal. Now your reactions slow as much as your pace.
You are {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB|Vulnerable} to Radiant damage, and you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ability checks and saving throws.

entities.name: Planar Binding (Fey)
entries: Your body and soul have been saturated with the magical energy of the Realm of Faerie. Rather than inuring you to the essence of magic, it has made you more susceptible.

After finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the first saving throw you make against a spell originating from an enemy.

entities.name: Planar Binding (Primordial)
entries: Your body and soul are connected to the Elemental Plane. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} as the plane attempts to pull you into it.

entities.name: Planar Binding (Seraph)
entries: Your body and soul are bound to the higher planes of good. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} as the plane attempts to pull your soul into its place of final reward.

entities.name: Plant
entries: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the Plant creature type. If you are already proficient in a skill when asked to make an Intelligence or Wisdom ability check relating to Plants, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} instead.
When you gain this feature, you also learn {@language Sylvan}.

entities.name: Poison Resilience
entries: Your exceptional fortitude lets you shrug off the effects of even the worst toxins. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against being {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}.

entries.name: Poison Indemnity
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you fail a saving throw against being {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Poison Tendrils

prerequisite.other: Stage 4 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}

entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison damage and are {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.

While your Writhing Tendrils are active, any creature you choose that starts its turn within 5 feet of you takes {@damage 3d6} Poison damage unless they succeed on a Constitution saving throw. The DC for this saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.

entities.name: Polyglot
entries: The advantages of mastering the languages of enemies and allies alike are clear to you. You learn two languages of your choice.

entries.name: Language Expert
entries: If you take this trait multiple times, you learn two new languages each time.

Additionally, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@action Influence|XPHB} action ability checks made to interact with another creature using any language you selected with Polyglot. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Possession
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you enter the space of a Humanoid or Beast and force that creature to make a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. If the target fails, you disappear, and the target is {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} and loses control of its body. You control the body without depriving the target of awareness.

While possessing a target, you can't be targeted by any attack, spell, or other effect. You retain your alignment, Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma, and any {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to having the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions. Otherwise, you use the possessed target's statistics but don't gain access to the target's knowledge, spellcasting abilities or {@action Magic|XPHB} actions, class features, or proficiencies.

The possession lasts for 1 hour, until the possessed target drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you end it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, or you are forced out by an effect that ends possession. When the possession ends, you reappear in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the possessed creature. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Power Nap
entries: When you don't know how long it might be before your next full respite, you learn to take maximum advantage of any rest you can get. When taking a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can choose to sleep for 1 hour. If you do so, you reduce your {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} by one level and regain a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} in addition to the other benefits of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Extreme Resilience
entries: If you take this trait twice, when using Power Nap, you can choose to regain a single resource that would normally refresh on a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. For example, a Sorcerer could choose to regain a Sorcery Point on a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Powerful Build
entries: Whether carrying well-earned loot or the body of a fallen companion, you shoulder that load with ease. You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift. A Small creature with this trait can use any weapon with {@itemProperty H|XPHB|Heavy} as long as they have proficiency with that weapon. (This is an Exploration trait.)

entries.name: Powerful Shove
entries: If you take this trait twice, you can move or knock foes {@condition prone} with ease. When you use {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} to shove a creature 5 feet or give it the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw. (This is a Combat trait.)

entities.name: Presto Powder
entries: For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls and you can take the {@action Dash|XPHB} or {@action Disengage|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entities.name: Primeval Body
entries: Your transformation to an Elemental creature has changed your body entirely. You no longer need to sleep, breathe, or eat. Also, you no longer age normally and suffer no effects of aging.

Additionally, choose one damage type from Bludgeoning, Cold, Fire, and Lightning damage. You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage type. If you already have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to it.

entities.name: Primordial Aura
entries: Creatures of your choice within 15 feet of you gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Cold, Fire, or Lightning damage. You choose the damage type after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, and you can change the damage type as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. If they already have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to any of these damage types, they gain {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to that damage type.

entities.name: Primordial Chaos
entries: The plane of Primordial energy that you're tied to strengthens its pull on you. Whenever you roll a natural 1 on a saving throw against a spell, you take {@damage 8d6} Force damage, which ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistances} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immunities}. This damage is in addition to any normal damage from the effect.

entities.name: Primordial Form
entries: You become an Elemental in addition to any other creature type(s).
Your Constitution score increases by 1, but cannot increase above 20 in this way.

entities.name: Protective Wings
entries: While you are manifesting your Angelic Wings, you gain the following benefits:

items: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, when you or an ally within 5 feet of you is targeted by a weapon attack, you can impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on that attack. If the attack hits the ally, you take half the attack's damage (rounded up) and the original target takes the other half.
You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws or ability checks to avoid having the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition or to escape a Grapple.
Your {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} is increased by 10 feet.

entities.name: Psychic Spirit
entries: Your strength of mind shields you from unnatural forces. You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage.

entries.name: Spirit's Strength
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you fail a saving throw against an effect that deals Psychic damage, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Pull of Oblivion
entries: Your connection to the material realm becomes gossamer thin. When you roll a natural 1 on a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you take {@damage 4d6} Force damage, and this damage cannot be mitigated. If this damage reduces you to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you die.
When you die in this way or any other, you can only be brought back to life through magic equivalent to level 7 or higher spells.

entities.name: Pull of the Netherworld
entries: Your new native plane attempts to pull you to it, laying claim to your form. Whenever you roll a natural 1 on a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you take {@damage 6d6} Psychic damage as the denizens of the fiendish plane attempt to unbind your soul from the Material Plane. This damage can only be taken once per {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short}, and it ignores all {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistances} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immunities}. If this damage reduces your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to 0, you die and your soul is immediately taken to the Netherworld. You can only be returned to life by spells of 7th level or higher.

entities.name: Purity's Pain
entries: Unspoiled, pure things cause you physical pain to witness. A child hugging their parent, a loyal dog laying its head upon a sick man's lap, a work of religious art devoted to an Arch Seraph, a baby's laughter: these things make you physically ill.

When you are in the presence of a pure act or object (GM's discretion) you are wracked with pain. At the start of each of your turns while you can see the cause of your pain, you suffer {@dice 3d6} plus your Charisma modifier Psychic damage, and cannot use a Hag Transformation boons until the start of your next turn. The first time you take Psychic damage from this flaw after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw. If you fail, you have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition as long as you can see the source of your pain.

entities.name: Queen's Command
entries: The Fey court lends you its power, and in exchange you must pay regular tribute to maintain your freedom. Twice a year, at times relevant to the Fey Court that you serve—such as the solstices or the equinoxes—you must deliver a treasure or other significant tribute to your ruling Fey Queen.

If your queen considers the tribute lacking, you are immediately notified of your failure and are given detailed instructions on how to atone. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on all {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests} until you rectify the situation. If you do not satisfy your queen's demand in one week's time, your queen transports you immediately to her Fey Court, where you must answer for your insolence. Once transported in this way, you must remain in the Realm of Faerie for 100 years, or until you strike a bargain with your queen to let you return to the Material Plane.

Your GM will tell you what constitutes an appropriate tribute to the court. Some Fey Queens prefer magic items while others wish for long-kept secrets, the capture of Fey criminals, or Humanoid servants.

entities.name: Quick Initiative
entries: Danger is never far away from you, and you are always ready for it. You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls.

entries.name: Focused Initiative
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can treat a roll of 9 or lower as if you rolled a 10.

entities.name: Quick Slip
entries: Even in the thick of battle, anything that obscures your enemies' view of you gives you a chance to strike unseen. You can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on each of your turns. You must have appropriate cover to attempt to hide, as normal.

entries.name: Astute Slip
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the Stealth checks you make with the {@action Hide|XPHB} action when you use Quick Slip.

entities.name: Ranger
entries: You always have the {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast this spell using a 5th-level spell slot without expending a spell slot.
Once you cast the spell using this talent, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Rapid Regeneration
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 5 Mutation Points}
At the start of each of your turns, you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} regained).

entities.name: Reach Attack
entries: As you hurl yourself into battle, your foes discover that trying to keep away from you won't save them. Your reach increases by 5 feet. This extra reach doesn't apply to {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

entries.name: Opportune Reach
entries: If you take this trait twice, your extra reach from Reach Attack applies to {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

entities.name: Reduce
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 1 Mutation Point}

Your size decreases by one category (from Medium to Small, for example). You can spend an additional 2 Mutation Points to decrease your size by two categories instead (from Medium to Tiny, for example). See the {@spell Enlarge/Reduce|XPHB} spell to determine the benefits gained from this ability.

entities.name: Regeneration
entries: The vampiric blood that flows in your veins gives you regenerative powers. You regain 15 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of your turn if you have at least 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} but less than 60 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. This feature has no effect if you are in sunlight or have taken Radiant damage since the end of your last turn.

entities.name: Regeneration (Armor Modification)
entries: You reinforce the armor with troll hide or soak it in vampire blood. You have a pool of six d10s. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a die from the pool, roll that die and add your Constitution modifier, and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll's total.
You regain all the expended dice when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Regeneration (Beast, Giant, Monstrosity)
entries: You regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d8} plus your Monster Hunter level.

entities.name: Relentless Endurance
entries: The battles you need yet to fight are many, and death is not an option. When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Unparalleled Endurance
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you use Relentless Endurance, you drop to {@dice 1d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. Additionally, when you use Relentless Endurance, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to spend up to five {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice}, rolling them and gaining that number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

entities.name: Resilient Ears
entries: Even as destruction rains down around you, your hearing stays sharp. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against having the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Determined Hearing
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Perception|XPHB} checks involving hearing. Additionally, when you fail a saving throw against being {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Resistance (Construct, Elemental, Undead)
entries: For 1 minute, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

entities.name: Rogue
entries: You've adopted the quick thinking of heroes who have outsmarted their foes. On your turn, you can take one of the following actions as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}: {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, or {@action Hide|XPHB}.

entities.name: Roiling Elements
entries: Your physical vessel strains to contain the roiling elements trapped inside of you. In a moment of distraction your body may erupt momentarily to reveal your Primordial nature. Tongues of flame may lash out or arcs of lightning might burst from beneath your skin.

You can contain the elements and maintain the appearance of the humanoid you once were, but this is taxing and requires effort. Moments of stress are likely to unleash your true nature, which might occur under the following circumstances:

items: Becoming {@condition Bloodied|XPHB}
Having the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition
Encountering Elemental creatures or powers
Having the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions
Using your Primordial Transformation powers

entries: In these events, or times of other extreme emotional or physical stress, the GM can call for a Constitution {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB} with a DC based on your current Transformation Stage:

When your Primordial nature is revealed, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on every {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} you make for 1 minute as you fight to control the volatile elements within you.

entities.name: Runic Bomb
entries: Runic Bombs are used to hunt creatures resilient to mundane weapons. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can throw the runic bomb at a point  within 60 feet that you can see, creating a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on that point. The {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} spreads around corners, and its area is {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB}. It lasts for 1 minute or until a strong wind (such as one created by {@spell Gust of Wind|XPHB}) disperses it. Whenever Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage is dealt to a creature inside the sphere, that damage is Force damage instead of its normal damage type.
A Runic Bomb is destroyed after a single use.

entities.name: Ruthless Response
entries: A creature that gets the drop on you is met with a swift and brutal reply. When you take damage from a creature within your reach, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a melee attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} against that creature. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Focused Ruthlessness
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls made using Ruthless Response.

entities.name: Sacred Retribution
entries: When an ally you can see within 30 feet takes the {@action Attack|XPHB} action with a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to imbue them with holy zeal, allowing them to make one additional attack. On a hit, the target takes an additional {@damage 1d8} Radiant damage. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Sangromancy Specialist

prerequisite.other: Stage 3 of the {@charoption Vampire|GrimHollowPG24|Vampire Transformation}, Spellcaster who can cast cantrips

entries: Your magic is tinged with the blood that you crave. You gain the following features:

items: You gain an extra {@dice 1d12} Sangromancy {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} per Vampire Transformation. You can use those dice on {@filter Sangromancy spells|spells|subschool=sangromancy}. You regain all spent Sangromancy Dice when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
When you successfully restore {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} using {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24}, you regain one spent Sangromancy Die.

Any cantrip you cast that does Necrotic damage can be enhanced by your Sangromancy Dice. You can spend up to 2 {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} (including your Sangromancy Dice) to deal extra Necrotic damage with those cantrips. You can decide to spend these {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} after you know if the cantrip affects the target(s).

entities.name: Savage Instincts
entries: You have developed an unrelenting thirst for bloodshed and carnage. While in your hybrid form, if you hit a {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} creature with a Claw or Bite attack, the attack deals an additional {@damage 1d8} Slashing damage.

entities.name: Scorpion Anchor
entries: This weapon is intended to keep flying foes anchored to the ground or stop monsters from fleeing. The Scorpion Anchor can be fired from a {@item Longbow|XPHB}, {@item Shortbow|XPHB}, or Crossbow as part of an {@action Attack|XPHB} action. When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a Scorpion Anchor, it has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition. A creature {@condition Restrained|XPHB} by the Scorpion Anchor can take an action to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against your Trapper Gadget save DC. If it succeeds, it is no longer {@condition Restrained|XPHB}.
A Scorpion Anchor is destroyed after the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} creature escapes or dies.

entities.name: Seasonally Affected
entries: Your connection to the Realm of Fey has a stronger pull on you. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} as the Fey Court attempts to claim your soul as their own.

Additionally, you gain {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to a certain damage type based on the Fey Court that you are pledged to. You cannot benefit from {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} or {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to that damage.

entries.name: Spring
entries: You are {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB|Vulnerable} to Necrotic damage.

entries.name: Summer
entries: You are {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB|Vulnerable} to Cold damage.

entries.name: Autumn
entries: You are {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB|Vulnerable} to Radiant damage.

entries.name: Winter
entries: You are {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB|Vulnerable} to Fire damage.

entities.name: Seraph Corruption
entries: The weight of evil in the world encumbers you more and more heavily as the days pass. Sometimes it becomes too much, and your celestial form is wracked with pain and doubt.
Whenever you roll a natural 1 on a saving throw, you suffer the following effects for 1 minute:

items: At the start of each of your turns, you take {@damage 1d10} Psychic damage. This damage cannot be reduced in any way.
You cannot regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or have {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.
You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the first attack you make every round.
Once per round, the first creature you force to make a saving throw against your spell has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the saving throw.

entities.name: Servant of the Autumn Court
entries: The Autumn Court embodies the careful scheming and approaching rot of the harvesting season. The Fey creatures of Autumn tend to be devious, scheming, and full of secrets. This is the court most associated with treants and other plant fey. Creatures of the Autumn Court often appear beautiful and helpful, but in truth their forms are hideous and methods vile.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. One creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of your starting or ending space takes {@damage 1d6} Poison damage. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Servant of the Spring Court
entries: The Spring Court embodies carnal desires, rebirth, and the planting and sowing season. The Fey creatures of Spring tend to be capricious, unreliable, and quick to flare their emotions. This is the court most associated with satyrs and nymphs. Creatures of the Spring Court hide savage intentions and uncontrollable urges behind gentle words.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. One creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of your starting or ending space takes {@damage 1d6} Thunder damage. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Servant of the Summer Court
entries: The Summer Court embodies the lush warmth and manic celebration of the growing season. The Fey creatures of Summer tend toward joviality, excess, and vanity. This is the court most associated with dryads and pixies. Creatures of the Summer Court often hide their narcissism and cruelty behind beautiful façades.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. One creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of your starting or ending space takes {@damage 1d6} Fire damage. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Servant of the Winter Court
entries: The Winter Court embodies the stillness, darkness, and death of the coldest season. The Fey creatures of Winter tend to be serious, cruel, and reflective. Creatures of the Winter Court are often terrifying to behold and very direct about their intentions.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. One creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of your starting or ending space takes {@damage 1d6} Cold damage. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Shadowsteel Absorption

prerequisite.other: Stage 2 of the {@charoption Shadowsteel Ghoul|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation}

entries: As the Shadowsteel courses through you, it begins to harden your flesh. Your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} increases by 1 when you aren't wearing armor.

entities.name: Shadowsteel Arcane Vessel

prerequisite.other: Stage 4 of the {@charoption Shadowsteel Ghoul|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation}

entries: When you cast a spell using a spell slot, the power of your Shadowsteel infection emanates from you. One creature targeted by the spell within 60 feet of you has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw to resist the spell, or you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the spell attack roll.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses after you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Shadowsteel Caster

prerequisite.other: Stage 2 of the {@charoption Shadowsteel Ghoul|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation}

entries: If you do not already have it, you gain the {@feat Shadowsteel Master|GrimHollowPG24} feat.

Additionally, when you use a Shadowsteel component to cast a spell using a spell slot, you can spend a Hit Die to regain that spell slot. The spell slot level cannot be greater than twice your Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation Stage.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your spellcasting ability modifier. You regain all uses of this feature after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Shadowsteel Curser

prerequisite.other: Stage 1 of the {@charoption Shadowsteel Ghoul|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation}

entries: If you do not already have it, you gain the {@feat Shadowsteel Adept|GrimHollowPG24} feat.
Additionally, when you cast a Shadowsteel curse, the casting time is reduced by half, and the saving throw DC increases by 1.

entities.name: Shadowsteel Explosion

prerequisite.other: Stage 4 of the {@charoption Shadowsteel Ghoul|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation}

entries: The Shadowsteel that suffuses your flesh responds to stress, and you can do nothing to control it. The first time you are {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} or reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} after you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you risk hurting everyone around you.

When these events occur, all living creatures within 60 feet of you must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, creatures in the area suffer {@damage 4d10} Force damage and have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of their next turn. On a successful save, a target takes half damage and is not {@condition Stunned|XPHB}.

entities.name: Shadowsteel Fury

prerequisite.other: Stage 4 of the {@charoption Shadowsteel Ghoul|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation}

entries: When you use the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to attack with your {@optfeature Shadowsteel Weapon|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel weapon} or {@optfeature Cursed Claw|GrimHollowPG24}, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make an additional attack with the same weapon or Claw. This extra attack carries with it the power of the Shadowsteel infection.

When you hit with this attack, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or gain a 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level. The saving throw DC is 8 plus the ability modifier used in the attack plus your Transformation Stage.

entities.name: Shadowsteel Weapon

prerequisite.other: Stage 1 of the {@charoption Shadowsteel Ghoul|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation}

entries: During a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you focus upon a Melee weapon within your reach, imbuing it with a fragment of your Shadowsteel corruption. This Shadowsteel weapon is considered magical.
When you reduce a creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} with a melee attack made with your Shadowsteel weapon, you gain {@dice 1d8} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} for 1 hour.
You can repeat the process described above to imbue a different weapon with Shadowsteel. This removes the effect from your existing Shadowsteel weapon.

entities.name: Shadowsteel Weapon Master

prerequisite.other: Stage 2 of the {@charoption Shadowsteel Ghoul|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation}

entries: You gain a +4 bonus to damage rolls made with your {@optfeature Shadowsteel Weapon|GrimHollowPG24|Shadowsteel weapon}.

entities.name: Shapeshifter's Savagery
entries: You have embraced the animalistic side of your transformation. While in your hybrid form, you gain the following benefits:

items: Your Claw and Bite attacks deal an additional {@damage 1d8} Slashing damage.
You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions.

entities.name: Shroud of the Wild
entries: With any degree of obscuration, your instinctive ability to conceal yourself lets you avoid your enemies' notice. You can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action even when you are only {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB} by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

entries.name: Faultless Shroud
entries: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Stealth|XPHB} checks using the {@action Hide|XPHB} action while using Shroud of the Wild.

entities.name: Silver Sensitivity
entries: You have developed a debilitating sensitivity to silver. In all forms, you have {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage from silvered weapons. In addition, you cannot have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to damage inflicted by silvered weapons.

entities.name: Skill Prowess
entries: Your ingenuity and inventiveness help keep you alive in a dangerous world. Before you make an ability check using a skill you are proficient with, you can add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} again. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Skill Mastery
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you fail an ability check made using the {@variantrule Skill|XPHB} Prowess trait, you can reroll the check and must use the new roll.

entities.name: Skirmish Tactics
entries: Your brutal strike leaves your foe reeling as you slip away. When you hit a hostile creature with an attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} against you by that creature have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} until the end of your turn.

entries.name: Supreme Skirmisher
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you hit a hostile creature with an attack with a weapon attack or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} until the end of your turn.

entities.name: Slimy Mien
entries: While manifesting your Ooze Form, you gain the following benefits:

items: You are {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition.
Your {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} range increases by 30 feet.

You can communicate with Oozes within 30 feet as if you shared a language. Oozes are not automatically {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} to you, but they do not regard you as food and may respond to simple questions if they are not {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile}.

entities.name: Slippery
entries: Any enemy that tries to grab you is in for a surprise. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Athletics|XPHB} and {@skill Acrobatics|XPHB} checks to escape a grapple.

entries.name: Supreme Slip
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you fail an {@skill Athletics|XPHB} or {@skill Acrobatics|XPHB} check to escape a grapple, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Slippery Ego
entries: As your brain becomes just one more part of the gooey mass that is you, and your sense of self becomes ever more tentative, the slightest distraction can cause you to forget who you were.

The first time you become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} after a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, or whenever you roll a 1 on a saving throw, the pain or surprise causes you lose your grip on your mind. You can't cast spells, activate magic items, understand language, or communicate in any intelligible way. You are overwhelmed with hunger and take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your next turn to make melee attacks against a random creature within reach. If there are no creatures within reach, you move until you are adjacent to the nearest creature, using the {@action Dash|XPHB} action if necessary.

You may attempt a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of your turns to regain your sense of self. This effect ends automatically when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Sluggish
entries: Your body frequently shifts and melts without control, making you sluggish. Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} decreases by 5 feet for every Transformation Stage you achieve.
In addition, your skin glistens with oozy droplets leaving a trail anywhere you go. Ability checks made to follow your tracks are made with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

entities.name: Soman Bloodline
entries: Your vampirism follows the Soman bloodline, the most prevalent in Etharis. You gain the following features:

items: Your Strength and Dexterity scores increase by 1. Neither score can exceed 19 with this feature.
You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls with {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24}.
The Necrotic damage dealt by {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} increases to {@dice 1d8}.
You gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

entities.name: Sorcerer
entries: Choose one of the following Metamagic Options available to the Sorcerer class: {@optfeature Careful Spell|XPHB}, {@optfeature Distant Spell|XPHB}, {@optfeature Empowered Spell|XPHB}, {@optfeature Extended Spell|XPHB}, {@optfeature Seeking Spell|XPHB}, {@optfeature Subtle Spell|XPHB}, or {@optfeature Transmuted Spell|XPHB}.
When you cast a Bard spell, you can apply that Metamagic Option as if you had paid its Sorcery Point cost.
Once you use this talent, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Soul Vessel
entries: You have successfully torn your soul from your body and trapped it in a suitable object. The object must be a trinket or item no larger than 1 cubic foot in size. This item becomes your soul vessel.

entries.name: Soul Vessel
entries: A soul vessel is an enchanted vessel containing a Lich's soul. Additionally, a soul vessel is a conduit for the lich to feed on captured souls. A soul vessel has the following statistics:

items: {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}: 18
{@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}: 10 x your Character Level
{@variantrule Speed|XPHB}: 0
{@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Damage Immunities}: Poison, Psychic; Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing from non-magical sources
{@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Damage Resistances}: Cold, Fire, Lightning, Necrotic, Thunder; Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing from magical sources

entries: While a soul is stored in your soul vessel, the soul vessel is considered charged. The soul contained within the soul vessel must be from a creature whose {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} is equal to or higher than half your Character Level. The soul vessel can only contain one soul at a time.
You can add a soul to the soul vessel 2 times for each of your Transformation Stages. You regain this ability when you complete a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

To store a soul within the soul vessel, the lich must use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action within 1 minute of a creature's death, or a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} triggered by the creature's death, while holding the vessel to capture the soul, and the dead creature'sbody must be within 60 feet of the vessel when the capture occurs. The creature cannot be returned to life until its soul is removed from your soul vessel.

If your soul vessel is reduced to 0 hit points, you must create a new one. Doing so takes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Until you do, you do not gain any of the benefits of having the soul vessel. Also, until you have a soul vessel, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the first {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} you make after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

If you are killed while you control a charged soul vessel on the same plane of existence, your soul vessel's charge is consumed. You are brought back to life as per the {@spell Resurrection|XPHB} spell within 5 feet of your soul vessel 1 day later. If you are killed and your soul vessel is not charged, you are resurrected 7 days later instead.

entities.name: Soul-Shattering Attack
entries: After you make a weapon attack or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can spend the charge in your soul vessel to add the {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} of the soul within it to the attack roll and damage dealt with that attack. If the creature dies from that attack, you can instantly recharge your soul vessel with the soul that you just expended.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to 4 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Spectral Form
entries: You are considered to be Undead in addition to your existing creature type(s). You cannot be excluded from Turn Undead.

Additionally, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage. If you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage from another source, you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} instead.
Finally, you stop aging. You are immune to any effect that would age you, and you cannot die from old age.

entities.name: Spellshine Ointment
entries: For 1 minute, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Force, Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant damage.

entities.name: Stalwart Reserves
entries: Each time you lay into a foe, their state of peril lends you vigor. When you hit a creature with a melee attack, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to roll a number of d4s equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the total rolled. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Stalwart Edge
entries: If you take this trait twice, you can take the maximum number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} rather than rolling.

entities.name: Standing Leap
entries: Threats on the ground are of little concern as you leap over them with ease. You can make a {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} of up to 20 feet and a {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} of up to 10 feet, with or without a running start. If your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is less than the distance you can {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB}, you can leap only a distance equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

entries.name: Incredible Leap
entries: If you take this trait twice, you can make a {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} of up to 30 feet and a {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} of up to 15 feet, as limited by your speed.

Additionally, when you jump out of another creature's reach, the movement of the jump does not provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} from that creature. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Steady
entries: No matter what kind of upheaval surrounds you, you stand your ground. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against having the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Stand Fast
entries: If you take this trait twice, standing from {@condition Prone|XPHB} takes only five feet of movement instead of half your movement.

Additionally, when you fail a saving throw against being knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Stealthy (Armor Modification)
entries: Your armor is draped with a shadowy cloak or made from hide as light as a feather. Your armor doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks, even if it would normally. While wearing your armor, creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks to see you, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks to notice creatures.

entities.name: Steelskin Ointment
entries: For 1 minute, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

entities.name: Strigoi Bloodline
entries: Your vampirism follows the Strigoi bloodline. Your speed and guile almost match your ferocity. You gain the following features:

items: Your Dexterity score increases by 2. Your Dexterity score cannot exceed 20 with this feature.
If you make your {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} attack with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}, the Piercing damage increases to {@dice 2d4} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier.
You can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, and you can use the {@action Hide|XPHB} action anywhere that is not in sunlight.
You have proficiency with the {@skill Stealth|XPHB} skill. If you are already proficient in {@skill Stealth|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in Stealth instead.

entities.name: Supernatural Hide
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 5 Mutation Points}
You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

entities.name: Supple Squeeze
entries: With an effort of will, you contort your body into the tightest spaces. You can squeeze through a space that is large enough for a creature two sizes smaller than you, rather than one size smaller.

entries.name: Full-Speed Squeeze
entries: If you take this trait twice, squeezing does not cost you additional movement, and you do not have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and Dexterity saving throws while squeezing.

entities.name: Supreme Sanguine Curse
entries: Your curse continues to transform you, bringing about further debilitations. You gain the following features:

items: If you start your turn in sunlight, you take {@damage 1d10} Radiant damage. You cannot have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} or {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to this Radiant Damage.

Your appearance begins to resemble that of a true vampire. In stressful conditions, as determined by you or the GM, your appearance changes outside of your control. When you do, anyone observing you knows your true nature.
You must feed every 2 days.

entries: You can hide your true appearance and disguise yourself as the humanoid you once were by {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on your composure. However, moments of bloodlust or stress are likely to reveal your true nature, including the following situations:

items: Becoming {@condition Bloodied|XPHB}
{@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on a spell
Taking Radiant damage from sunlight
Entering a feeding frenzy
Having the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition
Entering hallowed ground
Choosing to reveal yourself

entries: In these events, or times of other extreme emotional or physical stress, a GM can call or a Constitution saving throw with a DC based on your current Transformation Stage.  If you fail this save, your Sanguine Curse is revealed.
Non-evil creatures that witness your true form become instantly {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you, unless the GM decides otherwise.

entities.name: Swimmer
entries: You are in your element while in the water, moving with grace and ease. You have a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

entries.name: Quickened Swim
entries: If you take this trait twice, you can use the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while swimming.

entities.name: Telepathy (Aberration, Fiend, Plant)
entries: For 1 hour, you can speak telepathically to any creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself. Your telepathic utterances are in a language you know, and the creature understands you only if it knows that language. Additionally, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage.

entities.name: Tenacious
entries: Your enemies might put you down, but you are never down for long. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws}.

entries.name: Hard to Kill
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} but don't die outright, you remain conscious. You must make {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} as normal while at 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and you suffer a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB} failure each time you take any damage, but you can otherwise act freely. You can't become {@variantrule Stable|XPHB} while you remain at 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} in this way.

entities.name: Tenmen Tincture
entries: For 1 hour, you count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity, and when you attack with a Melee weapon whose mastery property you can use, you can replace that property with the {@itemMastery Topple|XPHB} property for that attack.

entities.name: Terrain Cloak
entries: Composed of local materials, a Terrain Cloak allows wearers to conceal themselves within the environment. Terrain Cloaks can be worn over Light or Medium armor and are donned and doffed with the speed of Light armor.
Creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks to see you.
The item lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, at which point the item falls apart.

entities.name: Terrifying Visage

prerequisite.other: Stage 3 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}

entries: Your mutations and unearthly appearance can unsettle even the bravest creatures. When you invoke your Aberrant Mutations or Writhing Tendrils, you choose one creature within 30 feet that can see you. That creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or gain the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The DC for the saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.
A creature that makes its saving throw is immune to this effect for 24 hours.

entities.name: The Green Sisterhood
entries: Green Hags are skilled deceivers who collect corrupting magic and secret curses. They use their magic to twist pure into foul, poking at the weaknesses of their foes. Green Hags embrace magical deceptions and are known for their vile recipes. There are no worse cooks in Etharis.

items: You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, its range increases by 60 feet.
You become Amphibious and can breathe both air and water.
You can mimic animal sounds and humanoid voices.

entries: A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a {@action Study|XPHB} action and a successful Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) check with a DC equal to 8 plus your Charisma modifier plus your Transformation Stage.

entities.name: The Red Sisterhood
entries: Red Hags are sometimes called the "nice" hags due to their ability to pretend to be helpful, but their kindness is a mask. While they are more subtle than other Hags and may choose to do good deeds, their motives are selfish. They delight in using honeyed words and offering deals that are just too good to pass up.

items: You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet.
You gain proficiency in {@skill Deception|XPHB} and {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}. If you are already proficient, you gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} instead.
You are immutable. No effect can change your form, and an illusion can disguise you only if the illusion gives you the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition.

entities.name: The Sanguine Curse
entries: The Sanguine Curse has taken hold of you. As a result, you gain the following features:

items: You cannot enter a private residence that you do not own without one of the occupants inviting you. Once invited, you can enter without penalty as long as the inviter resides there. If you do enter uninvited, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on all {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests} while in the residence. Additionally, you take {@damage 1d4} Psychic damage at the start of each of your turns. You cannot be {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistant} or {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to Psychic damage while inside such a residence uninvited.
You must feed at least once within seven days of your last feeding. See the Feeding sidebar for more information.

entries.name: Feeding
entries: As a Vampire transforms further and further from its mortal form, its metabolic requirements change. The first change is a need for mortal blood. This need is approximately 1 pint of blood from a Humanoid, Beast, or other creature with mortal blood.

As an action, you can bite a living creature within 5 feet that is {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by you, or that has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, or {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition. You drain the target of one pint of blood (or similar life-giving substance), leaving a visible bite mark on that creature. The bitten creature gains one {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.

A Vampire that does not feed within the required time enters a rabid feeding frenzy under the GM's control. They attack the nearest creature they could feed from. A Vampire remains in this state until they have drained a creature completely (killing them), at which point the Vampire falls {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} for 4 hours.

entities.name: The Sea Sisterhood
entries: Sea Hags enjoy spreading chaos. They hate order and calm, creating elaborate deceptions that lead to despair when uncovered. Their appearances are especially hideous, and their gaze can cause fear, or even kill. You gain the following benefits.

items: You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet.

You become Amphibious and can breathe both air and water. You gain a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. If you already have a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB}, it increases by 10 feet.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can gaze upon a creature you can see within 30 feet. If the creature fails a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to 8 plus your Charisma modifier plus your Transformation Stage, it takes {@damage 1d8} Psychic damage for each Transformation Stage you have.

entities.name: Timely Boon
entries: Fortune favors you at times when a threat might send you down. When you fail a saving throw, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to roll a {@dice d4} and add it to the save, potentially turning it into a success. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Born Lucky
entries: If you take this trait twice, you roll a {@dice d8} instead of a {@dice d4} when you use Timely Boon.

entities.name: Tireless
entries: An innate resilience lets you shake off conditions that would take others down. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws connected to gaining or removing {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels.

entries.name: Vigorous
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you fail a saving throw against {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Tooth and Claw
entries: You can manifest fangs, tusks, claws, or other means of harming your enemies. These manifestations also deliver a powerful magic jolt.
As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you manifest some natural defense mechanism for 1 minute. This gives you the following benefits:

items: You manifest a Claw, Bite, or Gore attack that you may use in place of any weapon attack. This manifested attack deals {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
After taking an {@action Attack|XPHB} action that did not use your manifested attack, you may take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} later in that turn to use your manifested attack once.

Once per round, when you hit a target with a manifested attack, you can choose to deal an additional {@damage 2d6} Psychic damage to the target. The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn. The DC is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.

entries: You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses of this ability after completing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Touch of Life
entries: Effects that corrupt the essence of other living creatures are of little concern to you. You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage.

entries.name: Strength of Life
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you fail a saving throw against an effect that deals Necrotic damage, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Toughness
entries: An intrinsic hardiness marks you as one born for battle. Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 each time you gain a level.

entries.name: Extra Tough
entries: If you take this trait twice, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by 2 instead of 1, and it increases by 2 each time you gain a level.

Additionally, when you make a saving throw against an effect that would decrease your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the save. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: True Name
entries: Your Fiend transformation is complete, and you are reborn. You are assigned a new name by the denizens of the Netherworld, which becomes your True Name. You receive a talisman of brimstone and brass with your true name inscribed in Infernal on it.

A creature with a {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} of 10 or higher within 10 feet of you that knows your True Name can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and speak your True Name. You must succeed on a DC 20 Charisma saving throw or suffer the following effects for 1 minute. You may attempt a Charisma saving throw at the end of each of your turns to end the effects.

items: You have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition, even if you are {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to being {@condition Charmed|XPHB}.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, the entity that has spoken your True Name can force you to move up to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and use your action to attack with a weapon, use your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, or cast a cantrip against a target of their choice.
All attacks against you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}, and you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws.
You cannot regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or have {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. You immediately lose any {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} you have.

entities.name: Twilight Glamour
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you gain the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition for 1 hour or until you end it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. If you make an attack roll, deal damage, or cast a spell that causes the target to make a saving throw, the remaining duration changes to 1 minute.
You can use this ability twice, and you regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Two-Faced
entries: You can transform your face into a vision of enchantment or horror that affects creatures around you, based on the Fey Court you serve. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you take on this visage. Creatures within 30 feet who can see you must succeed on a Charisma saving throw with a DC equal to 8 plus your Charisma modifier plus your Transformation Stage or be affected for 1 minute.
You can do this a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Spring
entries: Any creature that fails the saving throw has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition. The creature can attempt a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of their turns to end the condition. This effect ends early on a creature if you or an ally deal damage to it or take other harmful actions.

entries.name: Summer
entries: Any creature that fails the saving throw gains the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition. This effect ends early on a creature if you or an ally deal damage to it or take other harmful actions.

entries.name: Autumn
entries: Any creature that fails the saving throw gains the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. The creature can attempt a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of their turns to end the condition.

entries.name: Winter
entries: Any creature that fails the saving throw becomes {@condition Frightened|XPHB} of you. This effect ends early if a creature affected by this ability ends its turn out of your line of sight.

entities.name: Ultimate Brand
entries: When a creature affected by your {@optfeature Daemonic Brand|GrimHollowPG24} starts its turn, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to dictate its move and the action it takes. If you do, the creature repeats the initial Wisdom saving throw at the end of its turn. On a success, you can't use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} again while your brand lasts.

entities.name: Ultimate Predator
entries: You realize the true cost of your transformation. The beast within has gained more control of your body than you have. While you can control it at times, you know it cannot be contained forever—and when it breaks free, it delights in any slaughter it can find.
You gain the following features:

items: If you can see, hear or smell a {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} creature, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom saving throws.

At the start of your turn, if you can see, hear or smell a creature with the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition, you must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you transform into your hybrid form, or, if already in hybrid form you go feral as described under Lust for Hunt, above. On a success, you are immune to this effect until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Whenever you kill a creature while in your hybrid form, you cannot transform back into your normal form until dawn the next day.

entities.name: Ultimate Sanguine Curse
entries: Your transformation into a creature of the night is complete. And with that transformation comes susceptibilities. You gain the following features:

items: One strike may end your existence forever. When you are {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, {@condition Stunned|XPHB}, or {@condition Unconscious|XPHB}, and you are struck by a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} with a Piercing Weapon made of wood or silver, you must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you die and are reduced to ash. Only a {@spell True Resurrection|XPHB} or {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell can resurrect you.
You must feed every day.

entities.name: Unchecked
entries: Your ability to stay in motion is second to none, and foes try in vain to pin you down. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against being {@condition Restrained|XPHB}.

entries.name: Slip Free
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you fail a saving throw against being {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Undead
entries: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the Undead creature type. If you are already proficient in a skill when asked to make an Intelligence or Wisdom ability check relating to Undead, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} instead.
When you gain this feature, you also learn {@language Abyssal} or {@language Infernal}.

entities.name: Undead Form
entries: You become an Undead in addition to any other creature type(s). You cannot be excluded from Turn Undead.
You also stop aging. You are immune to any effect that would age you, and you cannot die from old age. You do not require air, food, drink, or sleep.

entities.name: Undead Resilience
entries: You become supernaturally tough to kill. When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} by any type of damage except Radiant, you can choose to be reduced to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead. Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Unholy Healing
entries: Your magical connection to your soul vessel restores your vitality quickly. While your soul vessel is charged, you can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to draw healing power from it. At the start of each of your turns for 1 minute, you regain 10 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. The soul vessel does not lose its charge.
You can use this feature three times, and you regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Unnatural Healer
entries: Your innate healing abilities let you recover from some of the grimmest wounds. During a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can automatically reverse {@variantrule Grievous Wounds|GrimHollowCG24}. Additionally, you can reattach any severed body parts (fingers, legs, tails, and so on), which are automatically restored at the end of the {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. If your severed body parts aren't available, you can replace them with the same body parts of another creature of the same general anatomy as you. If you wish to intentionally swap out body parts with replacements, you can sever your own body parts with no pain or discomfort.

The ability to make use of unusual body parts (for example, giving yourself the taloned paw of a lion if you lose a hand) are left to the GM's discretion. In any event, swapping a severed body part for an unusual body part grants you no mechanical Advantages not covered by other traits (see "Features and Traits" on page 11).

entries.name: Regenerative Healer
entries: If you take this trait twice, you automatically reverse Permanent Wounds during a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Additionally, you can restore any severed body part during a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, as if subject to the {@spell Regenerate|XPHB} spell. You can use this trait to create unusual regenerated body parts at the GM's determination.

entities.name: Unstable Existence

prerequisite.other: Stage 3 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}

entries: Magic causes your physical form to unravel. Whenever you roll a natural 1 or 2 on a saving throw against a spell, you reveal your {@optfeature Hideous Appearance (Aberrant Horror)|GrimHollowPG24|Hideous Appearance} and must roll again on the {@table Unstable Form|GrimHollowPG24|Unstable Form table}. If the result is less than your current Unstable Form effect, replace it with the new result.

entities.name: Unstable Form

prerequisite.other: Stage 1 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}

entries: Your body becomes malleable and struggles to maintain any one physical shape. Upon completing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} after an adventuring day in which you took damage, you must roll {@dice 1d100} on the {@table Unstable Form|GrimHollowPG24|Unstable Form table} and apply the corresponding mutation based on current your Transformation Stage. This mutation lasts until you finish another {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Untethered from Life
entries: Your life force is weaker than other creatures. When you would regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you regain half as many {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} as normal (rounded down).

entities.name: Venomous Attacks
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 2 Mutation Points}

Once per turn, a creature you hit with a Wild Shape form's attack takes an extra {@damage 2d6} Poison damage, and you can force a creature you hit with a Wild Shape form's attack to make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature is {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} for 1 minute. It can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition on a success.

entries.name: Repeatable
entries: You can gain this Mutation more than once, but no more than three times. For each additional 2 Mutation points, the attack does an additional {@damage 2d6} Poison damage.

entities.name: Viscous Durability
entries: Your mind and body become more Ooze-like. You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition and Acid damage.
When you gain this Boon, choose Cold, Fire, or Lightning; you gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the chosen damage type.

entities.name: Wall Crawler
entries: {@b Cost:} {@i 1 Mutation Point}
You gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your speed. For an additional 1 Mutation Point, you gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to twice your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

entities.name: Warlock
entries: You learn one {@filter Eldritch Invocation|optionalfeatures|feature type=EI} of your choice. If an invocation has a level requirement, use your Bard level.
When you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the invocations you know and replace it with another invocation for which you qualify.

entities.name: Weakened Constitution
entries: Your connection to the Fey Courts has granted you tremendous power but also an inherent fragility. When you become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} for the first time after you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you gain a level of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend a number of {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} equal to your Transformation Stage to remove one level of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} gained in this manner. You gain no other benefit from those expended {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}.

entities.name: Weapon Aptitude
entries: The weapons you wield might save your life one day, and you know their secrets. You have proficiency with three weapons of your choice.

entries.name: Weapon Specialist
entries: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain proficiency with three new weapons each time. Additionally, choose one weapon with which you have proficiency. You have a +1 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

entities.name: Weight of the Ages
entries: The weight of time is degrading for your form, and only the magic of your soul vessel holds it together. If your soul vessel is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, your soul is lost; you are killed instantly and your body crumbles to dust.

entities.name: Well Protected
entries: Your ability to roll with even the worst attacks means that armor would only slow you down. When you are not wearing armor, your AC is equal to 13 + your Dexterity modifier.

entries.name: Protective Cover
entries: If you take this trait twice, when you make a Dexterity saving throw or are targeted by a ranged attack, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the saving throw or impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the ranged attack roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Weretrap
entries: A Weretrap detonates when its fragile exterior is broken. You can take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to set the Weretrap in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. A creature within 30 feet must succeed on a Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) check against your Trapper Gadget DC to spot the trap. Creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this check.

A creature that steps into a space containing a Weretrap triggers the trap and makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 3d10} Bludgeoning damage and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.

The Weretrap can also be used as a {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} Ranged weapon with the {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse} and {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} property. It has a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. On a hit, the Weretrap deals {@damage 3d10} Bludgeoning damage and the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
The Weretrap is destroyed after it is triggered or thrown, regardless of whether it hits or misses.

entities.name: Wizard
entries: You prepare any two spells from the {@filter Wizard spell list|spells|class=Wizard} that are of a level you can cast. These count as Bard spells for you but do not count against your number of Prepared Spells.
When you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the Wizard spells you have prepared and replace it with another spell from the Wizard spell list of a level you can cast.

entities.name: Writhing Tendrils

prerequisite.other: Stage 2 of the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}

entries: You gain the ability to grow long, tendril-like appendages out of your body. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, these tendrils sprout from you. While you maintain these tendrils, you gain the following benefits:

items: When a creature moves to within 5 feet of you, you can try to push it back as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}. Unless the creature succeeds on a Strength saving throw, it is pushed 5 feet away from you and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn. The DC of the saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.
Your tendrils can protect you if you move. You can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to Disengage.
As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to being targeted with a melee attack, you can cause that creature to have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on all melee attacks it makes that turn.

entries: The tendrils last for 1 minute. You can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to retract your tendrils.

You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
